keyDecisionHeuristics: Match feature to license: P1=CA/dynamic groups; P2=Identity Protection/PIM/basic reviews; Governance=entitlement management, advanced reviews, Lifecycle Workflows.
Honor least privilege: prefer scoped roles, AUs, and PIM eligibility over standing tenant-wide assignments.
Connect for device writeback and PTA; Cloud Sync for disconnected forests and active-active HA.
App Proxy for HTTP/HTTPS; GSA Private Access for any-protocol VPN replacement.
Always exclude break-glass accounts from CA policies and protect them with FIDO2.
Global Administrator does NOT have Azure resource access by default — use Elevate access in Properties.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: Contoso has 25,000 users in on-premises AD. Security forbids any password material — even hashes — being stored in the cloud, requires real-time enforcement of on-prem sign-in hours and disabled accounts, has no AD FS investment, and wants minimal on-premises infrastructure. Which authentication method should you recommend?

options.A: PHS with Seamless SSO
options.B: Pass-Through Authentication with Seamless SSO
options.C: Federation with AD FS
options.D: Cloud Sync with PHS

questions.rationale: PTA validates credentials directly against on-prem AD via lightweight agents, stores no password material in Entra, and enforces real-time AD policy. Combined with Seamless SSO it provides desktop SSO with three small agents and outbound 443 only.

optionRationales.A: PHS stores a hash-of-hash in Entra, violating the no-cloud-hash requirement and offering no real-time AD policy enforcement.
optionRationales.B: Correct. PTA validates against on-prem AD with no cloud credential storage and enforces real-time AD sign-in policy via lightweight agents.
optionRationales.C: AD FS meets the security requirement but requires an AD FS farm plus WAP servers, contradicting the minimal on-prem footprint goal.
optionRationales.D: Cloud Sync supports only PHS, which stores hashes and does not enforce real-time AD policy — fails both key requirements.

references.text: Choose the right authentication method for Entra hybrid identity

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.topic: Hybrid identity — Connect vs Cloud Sync
questions.question: Litware has acquired two companies, each with a separate, disconnected AD forest with no trust. They want a single Entra tenant, multiple active sync agents for HA, and minimal on-prem footprint. PHS is acceptable. What do you implement?

options.A: Entra Connect in staging mode in each forest
options.B: Entra Cloud Sync with provisioning agents in each forest
options.C: Entra Connect with multi-forest topology
options.D: AD FS with multi-forest claims provider trusts

questions.rationale: Cloud Sync explicitly supports multiple disconnected forests and multiple active provisioning agents (true active/active HA). Microsoft cites M&A as the primary use case.

optionRationales.A: Staging mode provides active/passive HA, not active/active, and Connect only supports a single active instance per topology.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Cloud Sync supports multiple disconnected forests with multiple active agents simultaneously — Microsoft's documented M&A solution.
optionRationales.C: Connect multi-forest topology requires forest connectivity and a heavier on-prem footprint, and is active/passive only.
optionRationales.D: AD FS is a federation/authentication system, not a directory synchronization product.

references.text: What is Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync?
Cloud Sync vs Microsoft Entra Connect decision guide

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: You need a dynamic security group containing only Member-type users in Germany or France whose department is 'Finance.' Which rule is correct?

options.A: (user.userType -eq "Member") -and (user.country -in ["Germany","France"]) -and (user.department -eq "Finance")
options.B: (user.userType = "Member") AND (user.country IN ("Germany","France")) AND (user.department = "Finance")
options.C: (user.userType -eq "Member") -and ((user.country -eq "Germany") -or (user.country -eq "France")) -and (user.department -eq "Finance")
options.D: user.userType -eq "Member" -and user.country -contains "Germany,France" -and user.department -eq "Finance"

questions.rationale: Dynamic rules use PowerShell-style dash operators. The explicit -or form with parentheses is universally accepted by the rule builder and is the canonical exam-safe answer.

optionRationales.A: The -in operator with bracket-array syntax is technically valid in newer rule builders but is less universally accepted than the explicit -or form.
optionRationales.B: SQL-style syntax (=, AND, IN with parentheses) is invalid for dynamic group rules, which require PowerShell-style dash operators.
optionRationales.C: Correct. PowerShell-style dash operators with explicit -or and parentheses for country grouping is the canonical, universally accepted syntax.
optionRationales.D: -contains is a collection operator for multi-value attributes; misusing it on a scalar string produces incorrect results.

references.text: Manage rules for dynamic membership groups
Create simpler, more efficient dynamic group rules

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: Fabrikam University's Engineering IT team needs to reset passwords only for the ~4,000 Engineering students and faculty, with no tenant-wide admin visibility. What is the best approach?

options.A: Helpdesk Administrator at tenant scope
options.B: Create an Administrative Unit for Engineering and assign Helpdesk Administrator scoped to that AU
options.C: Password Administrator at tenant scope
options.D: Make them M365 group owners

questions.rationale: AUs scope admin rights to a subset of users. An AU-scoped Helpdesk Administrator can reset passwords only for AU members (non-admins), with no tenant-wide visibility.

optionRationales.A: Helpdesk Administrator at tenant scope grants password reset over all non-admin users in the tenant — far broader than needed.
optionRationales.B: Correct. AU-scoped Helpdesk Administrator limits password reset to only the ~4,000 AU members with no tenant-wide access.
optionRationales.C: Password Administrator at tenant scope is over-privileged and provides tenant-wide access.
optionRationales.D: Group ownership grants administrative rights over the group object but not directory-level password reset rights.

references.text: Administrative units in Microsoft Entra ID

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: Northwind is launching a retail mobile app where customers self-sign-up via email or social IDPs (Google, Apple), with branded sign-in, MFA, and 5 million-user scale, separate from the employee directory. Which service?

options.A: Workforce-tenant B2B collaboration
options.B: Cross-tenant synchronization
options.C: Microsoft Entra External ID (external tenant)
options.D: New Azure AD B2C tenant

questions.rationale: Microsoft Entra External ID (external tenant) is Microsoft's GA CIAM solution with social login, native CA/MFA, and scale up to 50,000 free MAU.

optionRationales.A: B2B collaboration is for known business partners requiring access to your apps — not consumer-facing self-signup at scale.
optionRationales.B: Cross-tenant synchronization pushes known employees between Entra tenants — it is not a consumer CIAM solution.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Entra External ID is the GA CIAM product supporting social IDPs, native CA/MFA, branding, and millions of users.
optionRationales.D: Azure AD B2C is closed to new purchases as of May 1, 2025.

references.text: Microsoft Entra External ID for customers (CIAM)
Azure AD B2C end-of-sale and migration guidance

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: A Windows 11 hybrid-joined laptop keeps prompting for SharePoint credentials. dsregcmd /status shows AzureAdJoined: NO, DomainJoined: YES, WorkplaceJoined: NO, and DRS Discovery Test fails. Most likely cause?

options.A: Entra ID license not assigned to the device
options.B: Missing or misconfigured Service Connection Point (SCP) in on-prem AD
options.C: User has not enrolled in MFA
options.D: Conditional Access is blocking the device

questions.rationale: A failed DRS Discovery Test on a domain-joined device that is not AzureAdJoined points squarely at the Service Connection Point being missing or misconfigured in on-prem AD.

optionRationales.A: Licensing does not gate device registration capability.
optionRationales.B: Correct. DRS Discovery failure on a domain-joined device indicates the SCP is missing or pointing to the wrong Entra tenant.
optionRationales.C: MFA enrollment is unrelated to device registration; it is enforced at sign-in, not join.
optionRationales.D: A CA block surfaces as a sign-in failure after registration, not as a DRS discovery failure during the join process.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: Developers must update credentials on all app registrations they own but cannot create new apps or change tenant settings. Built-in roles are either too broad or too narrow. Best approach?

options.A: Application Administrator at tenant scope
options.B: Cloud Application Administrator at tenant scope
options.C: Custom role with microsoft.directory/applications/credentials/update at tenant scope
options.D: Make them owners of every application

questions.rationale: Custom directory roles deliver least-privilege via granular permission strings. Requires P1. Built-in options all include create/delete/admin-consent — far too broad.

optionRationales.A: Application Administrator includes app creation, deletion, and admin consent — far broader than credential update only.
optionRationales.B: Cloud Application Administrator is similarly broad; excluding App Proxy still leaves create/delete/consent permissions.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Custom role with the specific credentials/update action string delivers exact least-privilege for this use case.
optionRationales.D: App ownership grants many additional permissions beyond credential management and doesn't scale to all registrations automatically.

references.text: Create and assign a custom role in Microsoft Entra ID

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: Contoso (parent) wants to push employees as users to Fabrikam (subsidiary) automatically and silently, where they appear as internal members for unified people search. What do you configure?

options.A: Cross-tenant sync provisioning job at Contoso targeting Fabrikam, Fabrikam enables inbound sync and automatic redemption, mapping sets userType=Member
options.B: Manual B2B invitations at Fabrikam with email verification disabled
options.C: Entra Connect with connectivity to both tenants
options.D: Microsoft Identity Manager (MIM)

questions.rationale: Source tenant (Contoso) runs the provisioning job; target (Fabrikam) enables inbound sync; both enable automatic redemption to suppress consent; Member userType supports the M365 multitenant-org experience.

optionRationales.A: Correct. Source-side provisioning job plus target-side inbound sync, automatic redemption in both tenants, and Member userType mapping is the complete solution.
optionRationales.B: Manual invitations don't scale, require knowing email addresses, and create Guest accounts rather than Members.
optionRationales.C: Entra Connect synchronizes from on-premises AD to Entra ID; it cannot synchronize between two Entra tenants.
optionRationales.D: MIM is an on-premises identity management product and is not supported for cross-Entra-tenant sync scenarios.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: You enabled Seamless SSO via Connect and pushed a GPO adding https://autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com to Trusted Sites. Domain-joined Windows 11 devices are still prompted on the corporate network. Why?

options.A: The Kerberos key on AZUREADSSOACC has not been rolled
options.B: The URL must be in the Local Intranet zone, not Trusted Sites
options.C: Devices must be Entra-joined (not just domain-joined) for Seamless SSO
options.D: The AZUREADSSOACC computer account does not exist

questions.rationale: Microsoft documentation explicitly states that placing the autologon URL in Trusted Sites breaks silent Kerberos negotiation; it must be in the Local Intranet zone.

optionRationales.A: Key rollover is a security hygiene practice but is not required for initial Seamless SSO function.
optionRationales.B: Correct. The autologon URL must be in Local Intranet zone — Trusted Sites uses different Kerberos negotiation behavior that breaks silent auth.
optionRationales.C: Seamless SSO is designed specifically for domain-joined (not Entra-joined) devices; Entra-joined devices use PRT-based SSO.
optionRationales.D: Entra Connect creates the AZUREADSSOACC computer account automatically when Seamless SSO is enabled via the wizard.

references.text: Microsoft Entra Connect Seamless SSO overview
Seamless SSO Kerberos decryption key rollover

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.topic: Connect vs Cloud Sync feature gap
questions.question: You run Entra Connect with device writeback (for AD FS claims) and want to migrate to Cloud Sync to gain HA and absorb a newly acquired disconnected forest. Can Cloud Sync fully replace Connect?

options.A: Yes — Cloud Sync supports all features including device writeback and PTA
options.B: No — Cloud Sync supports neither device writeback nor PTA; keep Connect for the original forest and add Cloud Sync for the new forest side-by-side
options.C: Yes, but you must federate first and then migrate
options.D: Cloud Sync is only for Azure AD B2C scenarios

questions.rationale: Cloud Sync does not support device writeback, PTA, federation, Exchange hybrid writeback, or custom sync rules. Connect and Cloud Sync can run side-by-side on different domains.

optionRationales.A: Cloud Sync explicitly does not support device writeback, PTA, federation, or Exchange hybrid — these are documented limitations.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Side-by-side is the supported pattern: Connect handles the existing forest with device writeback, Cloud Sync handles the new disconnected forest.
optionRationales.C: There is no requirement to federate first; the side-by-side topology is the documented migration approach.
optionRationales.D: Cloud Sync is a hybrid identity sync tool for on-prem AD to Entra ID; it has no B2C relationship.

references.text: Cloud Sync vs Microsoft Entra Connect feature comparison

questions.question: Contoso's CISO mandates that all Global Administrators use only phishing-resistant MFA, while other users may continue to use Authenticator push or SMS. Best approach?

options.A: Enable security defaults requiring FIDO2 for all users
options.B: CA policy targeting Directory roles → Global Administrator, requiring the built-in Phishing-resistant MFA authentication strength
options.C: Per-user MFA Enforced for admins plus tenant-wide SMS disable
options.D: Enable the legacy 'Require MFA for admins' baseline policy

questions.rationale: Phishing-resistant MFA strength accepts only FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business, or CBA. Scoping to the Global Administrator directory role cleanly limits the policy without affecting other users.

optionRationales.A: Security defaults apply tenant-wide to all users and cannot be scoped to specific roles or restricted to specific methods.
optionRationales.B: Correct. CA policy targeting the GA directory role with Phishing-resistant MFA strength enforces FIDO2/WHfB/CBA for GAs only.
optionRationales.C: Per-user MFA cannot enforce specific method combinations; SMS disable would affect all users, not just admins.
optionRationales.D: The legacy baseline policy was retired and is no longer available.

questions.question: A user receives a High sign-in risk after a successful MFA. The team wants ID Protection to handle these automatically — block at High, require MFA at Medium. Best configuration?

options.A: One CA policy combining sign-in risk High = Block AND user risk Medium = Require password change
options.B: One CA policy: sign-in risk High+Medium = Require MFA and Require password change together
options.C: CA policy on sign-in risk Medium+High requiring MFA via authentication strength, break-glass excluded
options.D: Enable both legacy Identity Protection risk policies

questions.rationale: Microsoft recommends CA-based risk policies and never combining sign-in and user risk in one policy. Password change is for user risk (credential compromise), not sign-in risk.

optionRationales.A: Mixing sign-in and user risk in one policy is explicitly against Microsoft's guidance; password change remediates credential compromise, not suspicious sessions.
optionRationales.B: Combining MFA and password change in one sign-in risk policy is incorrect; they address different threat models.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Separate CA policy for sign-in risk with MFA via authentication strength, break-glass excluded — matches Microsoft's recommended pattern.
optionRationales.D: Legacy Identity Protection policies are deprecated; missing break-glass exclusion risks locking out emergency accounts.

references.text: Require MFA for elevated sign-in risk
Require password change for high-risk users
Microsoft Entra ID Protection risk policies

questions.question: Help desk wants to prospectively simulate a sign-in for a Mac user against a CA policy without actually authenticating. Which tool?

options.A: Sign-in logs filtered by the user
options.B: Conditional Access What If tool
options.C: CA Insights and Reporting workbook
options.D: Policy impact view

questions.rationale: The What If tool is the only forward-simulation tool, accepting hypothetical user, resource, platform, client app, and location inputs.

optionRationales.A: Sign-in logs are retrospective — they show past events, not hypothetical future sign-ins.
optionRationales.B: Correct. The What If tool simulates a future sign-in scenario and reports which CA policies would apply, without authenticating.
optionRationales.C: CA Insights and Reporting workbook shows aggregate historical impact data from past sign-ins.
optionRationales.D: Policy impact view shows historical aggregate impact of a policy on real past sign-ins.

references.text: What If tool for Conditional Access

questions.topic: Identity Protection — leaked credentials and PHS dependency
questions.question: Contoso uses Entra Connect with PHS disabled (federated to AD FS). ID Protection flags a hybrid user with leaked credentials. The admin triggers a cloud password reset. What happens to the risk?

options.A: Fully remediated for both cloud and on-prem
options.B: Remediated only for the cloud session; the on-prem credential remains at risk because PHS is disabled
options.C: Leaked-credential detection does not apply to federated tenants
options.D: The admin must enable password writeback first before any remediation is possible

questions.rationale: Without PHS, Entra cannot validate the new on-prem hash. Cloud session risk is cleared but the on-prem credential may still be exposed. Detection works regardless of authentication method.

optionRationales.A: Without PHS, Entra has no way to confirm the on-prem credential has changed or that the new hash is clean.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Cloud session risk clears after the cloud password reset, but on-prem credential remains unverified without PHS.
optionRationales.C: Leaked credential detection works regardless of authentication method — it checks breach databases independently of PHS.
optionRationales.D: Password writeback enables pushing a cloud change to on-prem; PHS is what enables Entra to verify the resulting hash.

references.text: Microsoft Entra ID Protection leaked credentials detection
Password Hash Sync — how it works

questions.question: Users are prompted for MFA on every Outlook on the web visit. Admin wants prompts only on fresh sign-in but near-real-time revocation on password change or account disable. Which feature?

options.A: Configurable Token Lifetime set to 90 days
options.B: Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE)
options.C: Persistent browser session set to Always
options.D: Sign-in frequency set to Every time

questions.rationale: CAE issues long-lived tokens (up to 28 hours) to reduce prompts, and resource providers revoke them in near real-time on critical events like password change or account disable.

optionRationales.A: Configurable Token Lifetime is retired for refresh tokens and is not honored by CAE-aware resource providers.
optionRationales.B: Correct. CAE extends token life (reducing prompts) and supports near-real-time revocation on critical events.
optionRationales.C: Persistent browser session controls cookie-based browser session persistence; it does not address server-side token revocation.
optionRationales.D: Sign-in frequency = Every time maximizes auth prompts — the exact opposite of the stated requirement.

questions.topic: Conditional Access — filter for devices (PAW pattern)
questions.question: Privileged Access Workstations have extensionAttribute1 = 'SAW'. Allow access to Microsoft Azure Management only from these devices. Which CA design?

options.A: Require compliant device on Microsoft Azure Management for all users
options.B: Filter for devices in include mode (device.extensionAttribute1 -eq 'SAW'), grant Allow
options.C: Filter for devices in exclude mode (device.extensionAttribute1 -ne 'SAW'), grant Block, plus a baseline Allow policy
options.D: Add PAW IP addresses to a named trusted location

questions.rationale: Microsoft's documented PAW pattern uses exclude mode with not-equals + Block. This also blocks unregistered devices whose extensionAttribute1 is null — they fail the exclusion.

optionRationales.A: Requiring compliant device enforces Intune compliance but does not specifically verify the PAW identity via extensionAttribute1.
optionRationales.B: Include mode with equals only matches registered PAWs; unregistered devices have no attribute value and are not caught.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Exclude mode + not-equals + Block catches all non-PAW devices including unregistered ones where the attribute is null.
optionRationales.D: IP-based trusted locations can be spoofed and do not verify device identity or that the device is a PAW.

questions.question: A developer's Azure Function in two regions needs to read Key Vault secrets and write to Storage with no credentials in code, minimizing role-assignment overhead. Best approach?

options.A: System-assigned managed identities on each Function with separate role assignments
options.B: One user-assigned managed identity attached to both Functions, with role assignments done once
options.C: Service principal with a client secret stored in Key Vault, referenced in app settings
options.D: User credentials with MFA configured

questions.rationale: User-assigned MIs are decoupled from resource lifecycle and reusable across multiple resources, so role assignments are made once. Microsoft recommends user-assigned as the default for shared scenarios.

optionRationales.A: System-assigned MIs require separate role assignments per resource instance, duplicating management overhead.
optionRationales.B: Correct. One user-assigned MI attached to both Functions with role assignments done once minimizes overhead.
optionRationales.C: Storing a client secret in app settings is a credentials-in-config antipattern and requires rotation.
optionRationales.D: User credentials for background workloads cannot run unattended and require MFA interaction.

references.text: What is managed identities for Azure resources?

questions.topic: Conditional Access — combined session and grant controls
questions.question: A new finance app must enforce: compliant or hybrid joined device; non-persistent browser; reauth every 4 hours; break-glass excluded. Which CA combination?

options.A: Grant: Require approved client app; Session: Sign-in frequency 4 hours
options.B: Grant: Require compliant device OR Require hybrid joined device; Session: Sign-in frequency 4 hours, Persistent browser Never; exclude emergency account
options.C: Grant: Block; Session: App enforced restrictions
options.D: Grant: Require MFA only; Session: Defender for Cloud Apps Monitor only

questions.rationale: OR semantics between compliant and hybrid joined are supported. Sign-in frequency 4h and Persistent browser Never satisfy the reauth and non-persistent requirements. Always exclude break-glass.

optionRationales.A: Approved client app targets Intune-protected mobile apps, not device join state; doesn't address browser persistence.
optionRationales.B: Correct. All four requirements are met: device state (OR), 4h reauth, non-persistent browser, break-glass exclusion.
optionRationales.C: Block denies all legitimate access; app-enforced restrictions apply to SharePoint/Exchange, not custom apps.
optionRationales.D: MFA alone doesn't enforce device state or session persistence; MDCA Monitor mode doesn't enforce anything.

questions.topic: Per-user MFA to CA MFA migration
questions.question: Migrating from per-user MFA to CA-based MFA. Which statement is true?

options.A: Disabling per-user MFA clears all registered MFA methods
options.B: Per-user MFA and CA MFA can run in parallel without conflicts
options.C: Disabling per-user MFA does NOT clear registered methods; Microsoft recommends disabling per-user MFA before enabling the CA policy
options.D: CA-based MFA requires P2 for every licensed user

questions.rationale: Methods are attached to the user object and persist when per-user MFA is disabled. Running both simultaneously causes double-prompts and Intune onboarding failures.

optionRationales.A: Registered MFA methods are stored on the user object and are not cleared when per-user MFA enforcement is disabled.
optionRationales.B: Running per-user and CA MFA simultaneously causes double-prompts and breaks Intune enrollment flows.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Microsoft's switchFromPerUserMFA guidance: disable per-user MFA first, then enable CA MFA; methods are not affected.
optionRationales.D: Conditional Access requires P1; P2 is only needed for risk-based sign-in or user risk signals in Identity Protection.

references.text: Migrate to the Authentication methods policy

questions.topic: Azure RBAC — least privilege at resource scope
questions.question: Maya (Help Desk Operator) already resets passwords via Entra. The Azure team wants her to start and stop VMs in resource group RG-Helpdesk-VMs but never delete them. Best approach?

options.A: Add her to Global Administrator
options.B: Owner on the Azure subscription
options.C: Virtual Machine Contributor at subscription scope
options.D: Custom Azure role with start/action and deallocate/action (plus read), scoped to RG-Helpdesk-VMs

questions.rationale: Least-privilege Azure RBAC at the smallest scope. Entra roles do not grant Azure resource access — Azure RBAC is required separately.

optionRationales.A: Global Administrator is massively over-privileged and does not grant Azure resource access by default.
optionRationales.B: Owner at subscription scope allows deletion and all other actions across all resources in the subscription.
optionRationales.C: Virtual Machine Contributor allows VM creation and deletion and is scoped to the entire subscription — both too broad.
optionRationales.D: Correct. Custom role with only start/deallocate actions scoped to the specific RG delivers exact least privilege.

questions.question: An internal HR analytics web app reads Microsoft Graph nightly with no signed-in user. How do you configure permissions?

options.A: Delegated User.Read.All + Mail.Read; user consents on first run
options.B: Application User.Read.All + Mail.Read; Global Administrator grants tenant-wide admin consent
options.C: Linked SSO on the Enterprise Application
options.D: Password-based SSO with a shared service account

questions.rationale: Daemons use the client credentials flow, which requires application permissions. Application permissions always require admin consent and cannot be delegated to a user.

optionRationales.A: Delegated permissions require a signed-in user context; client credentials flow cannot use delegated permissions.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Application permissions (roles claim) with admin consent are required for daemon/background access to Graph.
optionRationales.C: Linked SSO issues no token and cannot be used to call APIs.
optionRationales.D: Password SSO replays HTML form credentials; it does not produce Microsoft Graph API access tokens.

questions.question: A vendor's multi-tenant SaaS requests Files.ReadWrite.All and Mail.Send. Users can't consent. Policy allows user consent only for low-impact permissions from verified publishers. How do you provide a self-service path?

options.A: Disable user consent entirely with no alternative
options.B: Enable the Admin Consent Workflow with designated reviewers
options.C: Add Files.ReadWrite.All and Mail.Send to the low-impact permission classifications
options.D: Convert the vendor app to single-tenant in your tenant

questions.rationale: Admin Consent Workflow lets users submit justification requests to designated reviewers, providing a controlled approval path without over-permissioning.

optionRationales.A: Disabling user consent with no alternative leaves users with no self-service path and requires admin intervention for every request.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Admin Consent Workflow (enabled by Global Admin) provides a self-service request + reviewer approval path.
optionRationales.C: Adding Files.ReadWrite.All and Mail.Send to low-impact classifications would allow any user to consent to highly sensitive permissions.
optionRationales.D: You cannot modify another organization's app registration to change its account type.

questions.question: An internal IIS intranet site uses IWA against on-prem AD. Remote Mac/iOS users need access without VPN; CA must enforce MFA + compliant device. Best approach?

options.A: Application Proxy with Pre-authentication = Microsoft Entra ID and KCD SSO
options.B: Application Proxy with Pre-authentication = Passthrough
options.C: Password-based SSO
options.D: Linked SSO requiring corporate network

questions.rationale: Pre-auth = Entra ID ensures CA (MFA + compliant device) is enforced before the connector forwards. KCD provides Kerberos SSO without requiring Kerberos on the client.

optionRationales.A: Correct. Entra pre-authentication enforces CA/MFA; KCD via S4U2Self/S4U2Proxy enables Kerberos SSO for the backend without client-side Kerberos.
optionRationales.B: Passthrough skips Entra pre-authentication entirely, bypassing all CA and MFA enforcement.
optionRationales.C: Password SSO is for HTML-forms applications; it cannot perform Kerberos delegation.
optionRationales.D: Linked SSO creates a launch tile only; it does not proxy traffic to the application.

questions.question: Block new client secrets on app registrations created after January 1, 2026, while preserving existing secrets and allowing one legacy app as an exception. How?

options.A: Disable user app registration via User settings
options.B: Default tenant app management policy restricting passwordAddition with restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime = 2026-01-01, plus a custom per-app policy on the legacy app
options.C: CA policy blocking password-based authentication for apps
options.D: Set 'Users can register applications = No' in User settings

questions.rationale: App management policies support prospective scoping via restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime and per-app exceptions for legacy apps.

optionRationales.A: Disabling user app registration restricts who can create apps, not what credential types can be used on existing registrations.
optionRationales.B: Correct. App management policies with date-based scoping and per-app exceptions are purpose-built for this scenario.
optionRationales.C: CA policies control sign-in authentication; they do not govern app registration credential management.
optionRationales.D: Restricting registration creation does not control which credential types existing apps can use.

questions.question: GitHub Actions must deploy to Azure without any stored secrets. Best approach?

options.A: Long-lived client secret stored in GitHub Secrets, manually rotated quarterly
options.B: Federated identity credential on the app registration trusting GitHub OIDC, subject = repo/branch, audience = api://AzureADTokenExchange
options.C: System-assigned managed identity on a GitHub-hosted runner
options.D: Self-signed certificate stored in GitHub Secrets

questions.rationale: WIF eliminates stored secrets by using OIDC token exchange. The GitHub OIDC token is validated against the federated credential trust and exchanged for a short-lived Entra access token.

optionRationales.A: Long-lived client secrets require manual rotation, are stored as plaintext, and are the antipattern WIF is designed to replace.
optionRationales.B: Correct. WIF is secretless: GitHub OIDC token + federated credential trust exchanges for an Entra access token with no stored secret.
optionRationales.C: Managed identities only work for Azure-hosted compute resources; GitHub-hosted runners are not Azure resources.
optionRationales.D: Self-signed certificates still require secure storage, rotation, and distribution — not truly secretless.

questions.question: A finance batch SP has had its secret leaked. ID Protection flags it High risk. Automated control to block future sign-ins for that SP only?

options.A: Disable the entire app registration
options.B: CA for Workload Identities targeting that SP with service principal risk = High and Block (Workload ID Premium required)
options.C: User-targeted CA policy requiring MFA from the SP owner
options.D: Add the SP to a managed identity exclusion group

questions.rationale: CA for Workload Identities supports single-tenant SP risk-based blocking with near-real-time enforcement via CAE. Requires Workload ID Premium.

optionRationales.A: Disabling the app registration blocks all tenants and all uses of the app — broader than necessary.
optionRationales.B: Correct. CA for Workload Identities with SP risk = High and Block action is the precise, automated, risk-based control.
optionRationales.C: Service principals cannot perform MFA; this control applies to human interactive sign-ins only.
optionRationales.D: Managed identities and service principals are distinct types; exclusion groups do not block SP sign-ins.

questions.question: Configure SSO with Salesforce from the gallery so users sign in with corporate credentials, CA/MFA enforced, with custom attributes (department, employee ID) in the assertion.

options.A: Password-based SSO
options.B: SAML SSO with Identifier/Reply URL, custom Attributes & Claims, certificate metadata exported to Salesforce, users/groups assigned
options.C: Linked SSO pointing to the Salesforce login page
options.D: Application Proxy with pre-authentication for Salesforce

questions.rationale: SAML 2.0 is the correct mode for Salesforce. Attributes & Claims maps directory attributes into the assertion. CA targets the Salesforce service principal for MFA enforcement.

optionRationales.A: Password SSO replays HTML form credentials and cannot carry custom attributes in a SAML assertion.
optionRationales.B: Correct. SAML SSO with configured Identifier/Reply URL, custom claims, certificate metadata, and assigned users satisfies all requirements.
optionRationales.C: Linked SSO issues no SAML assertion and cannot enforce CA or MFA.
optionRationales.D: App Proxy is for on-premises HTTP applications; Salesforce is a cloud SaaS app that does not need App Proxy.

questions.question: A developer added an optional 'groups' claim and a claims mapping policy, but the customized claims don't appear in the token. Most likely cause?

options.A: The user has not consented to the application
options.B: acceptMappedClaims=true is not set on the application manifest and no custom signing key is configured on the service principal
options.C: Claims mapping policies only work with SAML tokens, not JWTs
options.D: The app is registered as single-tenant

questions.rationale: Claims mapping requires acceptMappedClaims=true on the manifest (with verified domain App ID URI) OR a custom signing key as anti-spoofing safeguards.

optionRationales.A: User consent governs permission grants; it does not affect what claims appear in tokens.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Claims mapping requires explicit acknowledgement via acceptMappedClaims=true or a custom signing key — without these, claims are silently omitted.
optionRationales.C: Claims mapping policies work for both SAML assertions and JWT access/ID tokens.
optionRationales.D: Single vs multi-tenant registration does not inherently prevent claims mapping from working.

questions.question: Replace a legacy VPN. Users anywhere need on-prem SQL Server (TCP 1433), SMB shares (TCP 445), and RDP (TCP 3389). CA must require compliant device. Best approach?

options.A: Extend Application Proxy to publish SQL, SMB, and RDP endpoints
options.B: Microsoft Entra Private Access (GSA) with app segments for IPs/ports, GSA client deployed, CA requiring compliant device
options.C: Linked SSO tiles for each service
options.D: Header-based SSO with a third-party reverse proxy

questions.rationale: GSA Private Access is Microsoft's ZTNA solution supporting any TCP/UDP port, using the private network connector, with CA (compliant device) enforced via app segments.

optionRationales.A: Application Proxy supports only HTTP/HTTPS — it cannot tunnel SQL (1433), SMB (445), or RDP (3389).
optionRationales.B: Correct. GSA Private Access supports any TCP/UDP port and integrates with CA for compliant device enforcement.
optionRationales.C: Linked SSO tiles are browser-based launch shortcuts; they cannot tunnel non-HTTP protocols.
optionRationales.D: Header-based SSO with a reverse proxy handles only HTTP/HTTPS and cannot replace a VPN for TCP/UDP traffic.

questions.question: Investigate which apps users consented to over the last 30 days, including potential illicit consent grant attacks. Where do you look?

options.A: Service principal sign-in logs filtered by IP address
options.B: Audit logs → Category: ApplicationManagement → Activity: Consent to application / Add delegated permission grant
options.C: Risky users report in Identity Protection
options.D: Provisioning logs

questions.rationale: OAuth consent grants are recorded as audit events under ApplicationManagement, capturing who consented, to which app, and which permissions were granted.

optionRationales.A: Sign-in logs record authentication events; they do not capture OAuth consent grant events.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Audit logs → ApplicationManagement → Consent to application is the authoritative source for consent investigation.
optionRationales.C: Identity Protection risky users tracks compromised identity risk signals, not OAuth consent activity.
optionRationales.D: Provisioning logs track SCIM-based attribute synchronization, not OAuth consent or app permission grants.

questions.topic: PIM — eligible assignments with activation requirements
questions.question: Contoso wants 30 employees who occasionally need User Administrator privileges to have no standing rights, MFA on every elevation, manager approval, and 4-hour activation max. Best PIM setup?

options.A: Permanent active User Administrator assignment with CA-required MFA at sign-in
options.B: Eligible User Administrator assignment with role settings: Require MFA on activation, Require approval, Max activation duration 4 hours
options.C: Active time-bound assignment renewed every 4 hours daily
options.D: Eligible Global Administrator assignment with approval required

questions.rationale: Eligible + JIT activation with configured requirements is the zero-standing-privilege pattern. User Administrator is the least-privileged role for this use case.

optionRationales.A: Permanent active assignment gives standing User Administrator rights — no JIT, no elevation MFA requirement.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Eligible assignment with MFA, approval, and 4h max activation duration achieves all four requirements with zero standing privilege.
optionRationales.C: Active time-bound assignments still provide standing access during the time window — they are not JIT.
optionRationales.D: Global Administrator violates least privilege; User Administrator is sufficient for the stated task.

questions.question: 200 partners from 5 vendor firms cycle in/out of a 12-month marketing project; emails unknown up-front; access auto-expires every 90 days. Best approach?

options.A: Bulk B2B-invite all known partners and add to an M365 group manually
options.B: Cross-tenant synchronization from each vendor tenant
options.C: Entitlement management access package with 5 connected organizations, self-service request policy, 90-day expiration, and recurring access reviews
options.D: Self-service group join with no expiration

questions.rationale: Connected organizations enable external users to self-request without you knowing email addresses up front. The 90-day expiration and reviews provide automated lifecycle governance.

optionRationales.A: Bulk invites require knowing email addresses, don't scale, and lack automated lifecycle management.
optionRationales.B: Cross-tenant sync provisions users from managed Entra tenants with known identities — not open self-service for unknown partners.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Connected orgs enable self-service; 90-day expiration + reviews provide automated lifecycle with audit trail.
optionRationales.D: Self-service group join has no expiration, no access review governance, and no external user support.

questions.question: 4,000 guests; many haven't signed in for 6 months. Recurring quarterly control to recertify access across all M365 groups with automatic cleanup when reviewers don't respond.

options.A: PowerShell deletion of stale guests on a schedule
options.B: CA policy blocking guests after 180 days of inactivity
options.C: Access review on M365 groups with guests, group-owner reviewer, quarterly recurrence, Remove access on no-response, auto-apply, take recommendations
options.D: Lifecycle Workflow Delete user task scoped to userType = Guest, quarterly

questions.rationale: Built-in access reviews provide attestation with audit trail, quarterly recurrence, inactivity-based recommendations, auto-removal, and group-owner accountability.

optionRationales.A: PowerShell deletion bypasses governance, produces no attestation audit trail, and has no reviewer accountability.
optionRationales.B: CA blocking prevents sign-in but does not recertify access, remove group memberships, or delete accounts.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Access reviews with quarterly recurrence, remove-on-no-response, auto-apply, and recommendations satisfies all requirements.
optionRationales.D: Lifecycle Workflows automate JML tasks; they lack reviewer-attested recertification semantics for existing access.

questions.question: SOX access package: requestor justification, manager approves first, then app owner. Auto-deny on timeout. Configuration?

options.A: Single-stage approval with manager OR app owner as alternatives
options.B: Two-stage approval: Stage 1 = Manager (with fallback), Stage 2 = specific app owners; require justification; decision deadline with auto-deny
options.C: Lifecycle Workflow with two sequential approval tasks
options.D: Multi-stage access review with manager and app owner

questions.rationale: Entitlement management supports up to 3 sequential stages with manager resolution, fallback approvers, justification requirements, and auto-deny on timeout.

optionRationales.A: Single-stage with OR means both approvers receive simultaneously — no sequential ordering is enforced.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Two-stage sequential approval (manager then app owner), with justification and auto-deny on deadline timeout.
optionRationales.C: Lifecycle Workflows automate JML tasks; they do not handle access-request approval workflows.
optionRationales.D: Access reviews recertify existing access; they do not govern new access grant requests.

questions.question: A vendor needs Storage Blob Data Contributor on one specific storage account, JIT only, with manager approval and MFA. No standing privileges. Best approach?

options.A: Permanent group assignment of Storage Blob Data Contributor on the storage account
options.B: PIM Azure resource — Eligible Storage Blob Data Contributor scoped to the storage account; require MFA, justification, approval; max activation 8 hours
options.C: Eligible Owner at subscription scope
options.D: Active permanent assignment at resource group scope

questions.rationale: PIM Azure resource roles can be scoped to a single resource, providing JIT access with the least-privileged role and activation requirements.

optionRationales.A: Permanent group assignment creates standing Storage Blob Data Contributor access with no JIT or activation requirements.
optionRationales.B: Correct. PIM at single-resource scope with eligible assignment, MFA, approval, and max activation duration achieves all requirements.
optionRationales.C: Eligible Owner at subscription scope violates least privilege significantly.
optionRationales.D: Active permanent at RG scope provides standing access and is broader than the single storage account.

questions.question: When HR sets employeeLeaveDateTime, immediately disable account, remove groups/Teams, revoke sessions, remove licenses; delete account 30 days later. What do you build?

options.A: Two access reviews targeting the user
options.B: An Onboard new-hire Lifecycle Workflow
options.C: Two Lifecycle Workflows: a real-time leaver template (immediate tasks) plus a scheduled post-offboard workflow with Delete user 30 days after employeeLeaveDateTime
options.D: A custom Logic App that deletes the account immediately on an HR webhook

questions.rationale: LCW has real-time leaver and post-offboard templates that natively model the two-phase requirement. The 30-day delay is expressed as an attribute-relative schedule.

optionRationales.A: Access reviews are for recertifying existing access; they do not deprovision terminated employees.
optionRationales.B: Onboard new-hire is the wrong lifecycle category; this is a leaver scenario.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Real-time leaver handles immediate tasks; post-offboard with Delete user 30 days after employeeLeaveDateTime handles retention.
optionRationales.D: Custom Logic App loses the built-in audit history, 30-day retention period, and native LCW task library.

questions.topic: Diagnostic settings — archive and SIEM streaming
questions.question: Compliance requires 7-year retention of Entra sign-in and audit logs. SOC needs near-real-time streaming to Splunk. Most cost-effective combination?

options.A: Log Analytics workspace with 7-year retention
options.B: Two diagnostic settings: Storage Account (immutable, 7-year lifecycle) + Event Hub (Splunk add-on)
options.C: Event Hub only; configure Splunk to archive
options.D: Storage Account only; configure Splunk to poll blob storage

questions.rationale: Up to 5 diagnostic settings allow fan-out. Storage = cheapest immutable archive. Event Hub = canonical real-time SIEM streaming path for Splunk, QRadar, ArcSight.

optionRationales.A: Log Analytics at 7-year retention is significantly more expensive than blob storage for long-term archive.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Fan-out to Storage (archive) and Event Hub (real-time) uses two diagnostic settings and minimizes cost.
optionRationales.C: Splunk's own archive is not typically a regulator-accepted immutable archive.
optionRationales.D: Blob storage polling is not near-real-time and introduces latency for SOC SIEM use cases.

questions.question: Your deployment requires: Conditional Access, PIM with approval/MFA, entitlement management with connected organizations, and Lifecycle Workflows. Minimum per-user license?

options.D: Microsoft Sentinel KQL analytics rule on Entra audit logs

questions.question: A help desk technician needs to reset MFA methods for a user who is also a Helpdesk Administrator. Which role must be assigned to the technician?
