options.C: User Administrator
options.D: Helpdesk Administrator

questions.rationale: Privileged Authentication Administrator can reset MFA for any user including other admins. Authentication Administrator can reset MFA only for non-admin users.

optionRationales.A: Authentication Administrator can only reset MFA for non-admin users; it cannot act on other admin accounts.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Privileged Authentication Administrator can reset MFA for any user including those holding admin roles.
optionRationales.C: User Administrator can manage user accounts and passwords but does not have MFA method reset capability.
optionRationales.D: Helpdesk Administrator can reset passwords for non-admins but does not have MFA method management rights.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: Fabrikam and Contoso want to collaborate via Teams Shared Channels with no guest objects created in either tenant. Which feature should they configure?

options.A: B2B collaboration (standard guest invitation)
options.B: Cross-tenant synchronization
options.C: B2B direct connect with mutual cross-tenant access settings
options.D: Microsoft Entra External ID

questions.rationale: B2B direct connect is a mutual two-way trust used today only for Teams Shared Channels and creates no guest object in either tenant.

optionRationales.A: B2B collaboration creates guest objects in the inviting tenant — the requirement explicitly prohibits this.
optionRationales.B: Cross-tenant synchronization provisions users as Members in the target tenant — not for channel sharing.
optionRationales.C: Correct. B2B direct connect is the only feature designed for Teams Shared Channels with no guest object creation.
optionRationales.D: Microsoft Entra External ID is a CIAM product for consumer-facing applications, not inter-company collaboration.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: Contoso enables automatic redemption on its outbound cross-tenant access settings for Fabrikam, but Fabrikam does NOT enable it on inbound settings. Fabrikam users still see consent prompts when accessing Contoso apps. Why?

options.A: Automatic redemption requires P2 in both tenants
options.B: Automatic redemption must be enabled in both the source and the target tenant to suppress consent prompts
options.C: Consent prompts cannot be suppressed for guest-type users
options.D: The policy only applies to Microsoft 365 apps, not other enterprise apps

questions.rationale: Automatic redemption must be configured in both tenants — outbound on the source and inbound on the target — to fully suppress consent prompts.

optionRationales.A: Automatic redemption does not require P2; it is a cross-tenant access setting available with standard licensing.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Both the sending (outbound) and receiving (inbound) tenants must enable automatic redemption.
optionRationales.C: Automatic redemption is specifically designed to suppress prompts for cross-tenant guest and member scenarios.
optionRationales.D: Automatic redemption applies across all apps configured in the cross-tenant access settings, not only Microsoft 365 apps.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: You configured password writeback via Entra Connect, but hybrid users receive errors during SSPR. The Connect service account likely lacks which AD DS permissions?

options.A: Full Control on the domain root
options.B: Reset Password, Change Password, Write lockoutTime, and Write pwdLastSet on Descendant User objects
options.C: Replicating Directory Changes permission for PHS
options.D: Create and Delete User objects in the target OU

questions.rationale: Password writeback requires the Connect service account to have Reset Password, Change Password, Write lockoutTime, and Write pwdLastSet on Descendant User objects in AD DS.

optionRationales.A: Full Control on the domain root is unnecessarily broad and violates least privilege.
optionRationales.B: Correct. These four specific permissions on Descendant User objects are required for password writeback.
optionRationales.C: Replicating Directory Changes is required for Password Hash Sync, not password writeback.
optionRationales.D: Create and Delete User objects are not needed for password writeback operations.

references.text: Tutorial — Enable password writeback for SSPR

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: A hybrid user who is a member of the Domain Admins group in AD DS submits an SSPR request. The cloud reset completes but the on-prem password is not changed. Why?

options.A: SSPR does not work for hybrid users
options.B: Password writeback cannot write passwords for members of AD-protected groups (AdminSDHolder set)
options.C: The user's usage location is not set
options.D: Password writeback requires PTA, not PHS

questions.rationale: Members of AD DS protected groups (governed by AdminSDHolder — Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins, etc.) cannot have their passwords written back because AdminSDHolder strips the required permissions from the service account.

optionRationales.A: SSPR works for hybrid users — the cloud portion completed successfully, confirming this.
optionRationales.B: Correct. AdminSDHolder removes the writeback permissions from protected group members; writeback silently fails for these accounts.
optionRationales.C: Usage location affects license assignment, not password writeback.
optionRationales.D: Password writeback works with both PHS and PTA; the auth method is not the cause.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: An enterprise runs Entra Connect sync and three AD FS servers and wants Connect Health to monitor all agents. What is the licensing requirement?

options.A: Connect Health is free for all Entra tenants
options.B: A P2 license per monitored agent is required
options.C: A P1 license per monitored agent is required
options.D: Only the tenant administrator requires a P1 license; agents are free

questions.rationale: Microsoft Entra Connect Health requires a P1 license per monitored agent (Sync, AD FS, or AD DS agent).

optionRationales.A: Connect Health is not free — basic sync monitoring is included with Connect, but full health monitoring requires P1 per agent.
optionRationales.B: P2 is not required for Connect Health; P1 per monitored agent is sufficient.
optionRationales.C: Correct. A P1 license is required per monitored Connect Health agent.
optionRationales.D: Each monitored agent requires its own P1 license, not just the administrator account.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: A security team asks how quickly Entra ID reflects a password change made on-premises when PHS is configured. What is the sync interval for password hashes?

options.A: Every 30 minutes, aligned with the delta sync cycle
options.B: Every 2 minutes
options.C: Every 24 hours during a scheduled full sync
options.D: Immediately via Service Bus relay (same as password writeback)

questions.rationale: PHS synchronizes password hashes every 2 minutes, independently of the normal 30-minute delta sync cycle.

optionRationales.A: The delta sync cycle runs every 30 minutes, but PHS has its own dedicated 2-minute cycle.
optionRationales.B: Correct. PHS runs every 2 minutes independently of the main sync cycle.
optionRationales.C: Full sync runs much less frequently and is not tied to PHS synchronization.
optionRationales.D: Service Bus relay is used by password writeback (cloud-to-on-prem push), not by PHS (on-prem-to-cloud pull).

references.text: Password Hash Sync — implementation and sync interval

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: A user forgets their password and enters the same incorrect password 20 times consecutively. How does Entra ID Smart Lockout count these attempts?

options.A: As 20 separate failures, immediately triggering lockout after the threshold
options.B: As a single failure — Smart Lockout tracks the last 3 bad password hashes and repeated use of the same wrong password does not increment the counter
options.C: As 10 failures because only the first 10 attempts per hour are counted
options.D: Smart Lockout is disabled for repeated identical passwords

questions.rationale: Smart Lockout tracks the last 3 bad password hashes. Repeated use of the same wrong password is counted as one failure, preventing accidental lockout from stale cached passwords.

optionRationales.A: Smart Lockout deduplicates identical bad passwords — 20 identical attempts count as one failure.
optionRationales.B: Correct. The last 3 bad password hashes are tracked; the same wrong password counts as one failure regardless of how many times it is entered.
optionRationales.C: There is no per-hour attempt cap; the deduplication is based on identical hash matching.
optionRationales.D: Smart Lockout is always active; repeated identical passwords are counted as one failure, not disabled.

references.text: Smart Lockout in Microsoft Entra ID

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: Security policy requires regular rotation of Kerberos decryption keys. For the AZUREADSSOACC computer account used by Seamless SSO, what rollover frequency does Microsoft recommend?

options.A: Never — the key never expires and cannot be rolled
options.B: Every 90 days, aligned with password rotation policies
options.C: Every 30 days
options.D: Only when the account password has expired

questions.rationale: Microsoft recommends rolling the Kerberos decryption key on the AZUREADSSOACC computer account every 30 days, done via the Connect wizard or PowerShell.

optionRationales.A: The key can and should be rolled; it does not expire automatically but should be rotated for security hygiene.
optionRationales.B: 90 days is a common password rotation interval but Microsoft's recommendation for the AZUREADSSOACC key is 30 days.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Microsoft's documented recommendation is to roll the AZUREADSSOACC Kerberos key every 30 days.
optionRationales.D: The AZUREADSSOACC account password is separate from the Kerberos decryption key; rolling the key does not depend on password expiry.

questions.question: A new employee is onboarding remotely and needs to register a FIDO2 security key. They have no existing MFA methods. What authentication method should the admin provision to bootstrap this?

options.A: A permanent password reset and SMS verification
options.B: A Temporary Access Pass (TAP)
options.C: An Authenticator push notification via the combined registration portal
options.D: A client certificate issued by the on-prem CA

questions.rationale: TAP is specifically designed for bootstrap scenarios — a time-limited passcode that allows registration of stronger methods like FIDO2 without requiring existing MFA.

optionRationales.A: SMS requires prior phone number registration; it cannot bootstrap an account with no existing methods.
optionRationales.B: Correct. TAP is purpose-built for onboarding scenarios where no prior MFA method exists.
optionRationales.C: Authenticator push requires prior app installation and device registration — cannot be used without existing setup.
optionRationales.D: Client certificates require an existing enrollment infrastructure and cannot be self-provisioned in this scenario.

questions.question: A security policy prohibits email OTP and security questions for any purpose other than SSPR. Is this consistent with how the unified Authentication methods policy classifies these methods?

options.A: No — email OTP can also be used as a primary authentication method for guests
options.B: Yes — email OTP and security questions are SSPR-only and cannot be used for MFA or primary auth
options.C: No — security questions can be used as a second factor in MFA policies
options.D: No — email OTP is a valid MFA method for all users when enabled in the policy

questions.rationale: In the unified Authentication methods policy, email OTP is SSPR-only and security questions are SSPR-only. Neither can be used as an MFA factor or primary authentication method.

optionRationales.A: Email OTP is classified as SSPR-only in the unified policy; it cannot be used for primary authentication.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Both email OTP and security questions are SSPR-only methods in the unified Authentication methods policy.
optionRationales.C: Security questions are explicitly limited to SSPR and cannot be used as an MFA factor.
optionRationales.D: Email OTP is SSPR-only regardless of whether it is enabled in the policy.

questions.question: An admin configures SSPR and a Global Administrator tries to reset using a single security question as their method. What happens?

options.A: The reset succeeds; admins use the same SSPR policy as users
options.B: The reset fails — admins always require two methods for SSPR and security questions are not permitted for admin accounts
options.C: The reset succeeds because Global Admins bypass SSPR method restrictions
options.D: The reset requires approval from a Privileged Role Administrator

questions.rationale: SSPR for admin accounts always requires two authentication methods and explicitly excludes security questions. This is a Microsoft hardcoded policy that cannot be changed.

optionRationales.A: Admin accounts have stricter SSPR requirements than regular users — two methods always required, security questions excluded.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Two-method requirement and security question exclusion for admin SSPR is a hardcoded Microsoft policy.
optionRationales.C: Global Admins cannot bypass this restriction — it is enforced by Microsoft and cannot be changed by tenant configuration.
optionRationales.D: There is no approval workflow for SSPR; the requirement is method count and type, not approval.

questions.question: A user needs to register both MFA and SSPR authentication methods. Which URL should the help desk direct them to?

options.A: https://account.activedirectory.windowsazure.com/proofup.aspx
options.B: https://mysignins.microsoft.com/security-info
options.C: https://myapps.microsoft.com
options.D: https://portal.azure.com/#blade/Microsoft_AAD_IAM/AuthenticationMethodsMenuBlade

questions.rationale: Combined security information registration is at https://mysignins.microsoft.com/security-info, where users register both MFA and SSPR methods in a single flow.

optionRationales.A: The proofup.aspx URL is a legacy MFA registration page and is not the combined registration experience.
optionRationales.B: Correct. https://mysignins.microsoft.com/security-info is the combined MFA + SSPR registration portal.
optionRationales.C: https://myapps.microsoft.com is the app launcher for SSO apps, not the authentication method registration portal.
optionRationales.D: The Azure portal blade is for admin configuration of authentication methods, not user self-registration.

references.text: Combined registration for SSPR and MFA

questions.question: Your organization wants to add 'Northwind' to the custom banned password list. What are the constraints on the custom list?

options.A: Up to 500 terms, minimum 8 characters, case-sensitive
options.B: Up to 1,000 case-insensitive terms, each between 4 and 16 characters; character substitution detection applies
options.C: Unlimited terms; each must exactly match a substring of the password
options.D: Up to 100 terms; only whole-word matches, no substitution detection

questions.rationale: The custom banned list supports up to 1,000 case-insensitive terms between 4 and 16 characters. Substitution detection (o→0, a→@) catches variants. Requires P1 (P2 for on-prem agents).

optionRationales.A: The limit is 1,000 terms (not 500), minimum 4 characters (not 8), and the list is case-insensitive.
optionRationales.B: Correct. 1,000 case-insensitive terms, 4-16 characters, with substitution detection catching variants like N0rthw1nd.
optionRationales.C: There is a 1,000-term limit; the list is not unlimited.
optionRationales.D: The term limit is 1,000 and substitution detection is a key feature of the custom banned list.

references.text: Microsoft Entra Password Protection custom banned list

questions.question: An admin asks about Entra ID Smart Lockout's default configuration. Which values are correct?

options.A: 5 failed attempts, 30-second initial lockout duration
options.B: 10 failed attempts, 60-second initial lockout duration
options.C: 15 failed attempts, 120-second lockout duration
options.D: 3 failed attempts, permanent lockout until admin intervention

questions.rationale: Smart Lockout defaults are 10 failed attempts (3 in US Government Cloud) and a 60-second initial lockout duration that increases with each subsequent lockout.

optionRationales.A: The default threshold is 10 failed attempts (not 5) and the initial lockout is 60 seconds (not 30).
optionRationales.B: Correct. 10 failed attempts and 60-second initial lockout are the documented Entra ID Smart Lockout defaults.
optionRationales.C: The threshold is 10 (not 15) and the initial lockout is 60 seconds (not 120).
optionRationales.D: 3 failed attempts is the US Government Cloud threshold; standard tenants default to 10; lockout is never permanent.

questions.question: Contoso uses Entra ID with PHS and on-prem AD DS has its own lockout policy. How should Smart Lockout thresholds be configured to avoid inconsistencies?

options.A: Set both thresholds identically so they lock out simultaneously
options.B: Set the Entra Smart Lockout threshold lower than the AD DS threshold, and the Entra lockout duration longer than AD DS
options.C: Disable AD DS lockout and rely entirely on Entra Smart Lockout
options.D: Set the AD DS threshold lower than Entra so on-prem locks first

questions.rationale: Microsoft's hybrid guidance: set the Entra Smart Lockout threshold lower than AD DS and the Entra duration longer than AD DS, so Entra locks first and prevents AD DS from locking.

optionRationales.A: Identical thresholds can cause race conditions between cloud and on-prem lockout; Microsoft recommends a deliberate offset.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Entra threshold lower + Entra duration longer ensures Entra always locks before AD DS, preventing on-prem lockout.
optionRationales.C: Disabling AD DS lockout removes a critical on-prem security control; not recommended.
optionRationales.D: If AD DS locks first, users cannot authenticate via PTA or domain logon; Entra should lock first.

questions.question: A CA policy must require only phishing-resistant methods. Which built-in authentication strength covers FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business, and CBA — and nothing else?

options.A: Multifactor authentication
options.B: Passwordless MFA
options.C: Phishing-resistant MFA
options.D: Strong authentication

questions.rationale: Phishing-resistant MFA is the built-in strength that includes only FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business, and CBA — all three are phishing-resistant.

optionRationales.A: Multifactor authentication includes all MFA methods including push notifications, OTP, and SMS — not phishing-resistant only.
optionRationales.B: Passwordless MFA includes phishing-resistant methods plus Authenticator passwordless phone sign-in, which is not phishing-resistant.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Phishing-resistant MFA includes only FIDO2, WHfB, and CBA — exactly the three phishing-resistant methods.
optionRationales.D: 'Strong authentication' is not a built-in authentication strength name in Entra ID.

questions.question: An admin creates a new CA policy and sets it to Report-only. Which statement is true?

options.A: Report-only policies block access but suppress notifications
options.B: Report-only policies are evaluated and results logged to sign-in logs without affecting access
options.C: Report-only mode is only available for policies targeting All users
options.D: Report-only policies automatically enable after 30 days

questions.rationale: Report-only mode evaluates the policy against real sign-ins and writes results to sign-in logs without enforcing access decisions. It is the default state for new CA policies.

optionRationales.A: Report-only mode does not block access; it is a read-only evaluation mode with no enforcement.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Report-only evaluates and logs without enforcing — the default state for new policies.
optionRationales.C: Report-only is available for any policy scope, not only All users.
optionRationales.D: There is no auto-enable timer; report-only policies remain in that state until an admin manually changes them.

questions.question: An admin configures a CA policy on the user action 'Register security information' and wants to test it with the What If tool. What should they be aware of?

options.A: The What If tool fully supports user-action scopes and returns accurate results
options.B: The What If tool does not apply to user-action scopes — simulations only work for resource (app) targets
options.C: What If only supports simulation of Microsoft first-party apps
options.D: What If requires report-only mode to be enabled on the policy first

questions.rationale: The CA What If tool does not support user-action scopes (Register security info, Register or join devices). It works only for resource targets.

optionRationales.A: User-action scopes are a documented limitation of the What If tool — results for these scopes are not returned.
optionRationales.B: Correct. What If only simulates policies targeting cloud apps, authentication contexts, and traffic profiles — not user actions.
optionRationales.C: What If supports any cloud app target, not only Microsoft first-party apps.
optionRationales.D: What If does not require report-only mode; it can simulate enabled policies.

references.text: Conditional Access What If tool — limitations

questions.question: Microsoft auto-deploys a CA policy requiring MFA for admins to Contoso's tenant. The security team wants to modify which users are excluded. Is this possible?

options.A: Microsoft-managed policies cannot be modified at all; they must be disabled to deviate
options.B: Microsoft-managed policies allow editing excluded users and toggling state (on/off/report-only) but not changing core conditions
options.C: Microsoft-managed policies can be fully edited like any custom policy
options.D: Microsoft-managed policies only apply to tenants with no custom CA policies

questions.rationale: Microsoft-managed policies allow admins to edit the excluded users list and toggle state (enabled/report-only/disabled) but not change core assignments or controls.

optionRationales.A: Microsoft-managed policies can be modified in limited ways — excluded users and state can be changed without disabling.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Excluded users and policy state are the only editable elements in Microsoft-managed policies.
optionRationales.C: Core assignments and access controls in Microsoft-managed policies cannot be modified.
optionRationales.D: Microsoft-managed policies deploy to all eligible tenants regardless of their custom CA policy count.

questions.question: You want to enforce MFA for any access to the Entra admin center, Azure portal, Microsoft 365 admin center, and other Microsoft administration portals in a single CA policy. What do you select as the target resource?

options.A: Select each portal individually by app ID
options.B: Microsoft Admin Portals — a built-in grouping covering all Microsoft administration surfaces
options.C: All cloud apps, with an exclusion list for non-admin apps
options.D: Office 365 as the app grouping

questions.rationale: 'Microsoft Admin Portals' is a dedicated CA target resource grouping that covers all Microsoft administration surfaces in one selection, including future portals.

optionRationales.A: Selecting each portal individually is error-prone and will miss new portals added in the future.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Microsoft Admin Portals is a built-in grouping that includes all Microsoft admin surfaces automatically.
optionRationales.C: All cloud apps is broader than required and would affect non-admin apps unnecessarily.
optionRationales.D: Office 365 is a different app grouping that covers productivity apps, not administration portals.

references.text: Conditional Access — Require MFA for admins accessing admin portals

questions.question: ID Protection flags a user with a 'Leaked credentials' detection. What risk level is assigned and what is required to remediate it for a hybrid user?

options.A: Medium risk; remediated by completing MFA
options.B: High risk; requires a secure password change and PHS to confirm on-prem remediation
options.C: Low risk; system auto-remediates within 24 hours
options.D: High risk; the user must re-register all MFA methods

questions.rationale: Leaked credentials detection is always High risk. Remediation requires a secure password change. For hybrid users, PHS must be enabled so Entra can verify the new on-prem hash.

optionRationales.A: Leaked credentials is always High risk, not Medium; MFA completion dismisses sign-in risk, not user risk.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Always High risk; remediation requires secure password change; PHS required for hybrid on-prem verification.
optionRationales.C: Leaked credentials is always High and does not auto-remediate — user or admin action is required.
optionRationales.D: MFA re-registration is not the remediation action for leaked credentials; secure password change is required.

references.text: Microsoft Entra ID Protection risk detections

questions.question: A security analyst reviews a risky sign-in and confirms it was legitimate travel by the user. What action improves detection accuracy?

options.A: Dismiss user risk to reset the risk state
options.B: Confirm sign-in safe — this trains the ID Protection model and reduces future false positives
options.C: Confirm user compromised to trigger remediation policies
options.D: Reset the user's password to force a credential change

questions.rationale: Confirming a sign-in as safe trains the ID Protection ML model and helps reduce false positives for similar future sign-in patterns.

optionRationales.A: Dismissing user risk resets risk state but provides no model feedback to reduce future false positives.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Confirm sign-in safe provides ML feedback and improves future detection accuracy for this user's patterns.
optionRationales.C: Confirming compromised sets risk to High and triggers remediation — the opposite of what is needed for a false positive.
optionRationales.D: Password reset is a remediation action for user risk, not the appropriate response to a false positive sign-in risk.

questions.question: Fabrikam wants to replace its legacy web filtering proxy with a Microsoft-native identity-aware SWG with URL filtering and Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps integration. Which GSA product covers this?

options.A: Microsoft Entra Private Access
options.B: Microsoft Entra Internet Access
options.C: Microsoft Entra Application Proxy
options.D: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (CASB only)

questions.rationale: Microsoft Entra Internet Access is the identity-aware Secure Web Gateway component of GSA with web content filtering and Defender for Cloud Apps integration.

optionRationales.A: Entra Private Access is the ZTNA product for replacing VPNs — it does not provide SWG or web filtering.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Entra Internet Access is the GSA SWG with URL/web content filtering and MDCA integration.
optionRationales.C: Application Proxy publishes on-prem HTTP applications; it is not a web filtering proxy.
optionRationales.D: Defender for Cloud Apps is a CASB for SaaS app visibility and control — not a full SWG with egress filtering.

references.text: Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access overview

questions.question: Contoso has deployed the GSA client on all managed devices. They want CA to require that all access to sensitive SharePoint sites flows through the GSA tunnel. Which CA condition achieves this?

options.A: Filter for devices: device.isCompliant -eq True
options.B: Named locations: include only the GSA egress IP ranges
options.C: Compliant Network condition in the CA policy
options.D: Require hybrid joined device in the grant controls

questions.rationale: The Compliant Network CA condition checks whether traffic flows through the Global Secure Access tunnel, gating access based on network path rather than just device state or IP.

optionRationales.A: Device compliance checks Intune compliance state but does not verify whether traffic is routed through the GSA tunnel.
optionRationales.B: Named locations based on GSA egress IPs can be spoofed if an attacker knows the IP ranges.
optionRationales.C: Correct. The Compliant Network condition verifies the GSA tunnel is in use — the definitive signal for GSA-routed traffic.
optionRationales.D: Hybrid join verifies device registration state, not the network path through GSA.

questions.question: Your organization wants to use Azure RBAC to control access to Key Vault secrets instead of vault access policies. Is this supported and recommended?

options.A: No — Key Vault only supports vault access policies for secrets
options.B: Yes — Azure RBAC is the recommended access model for Key Vault, supporting fine-grained control at the vault, secret, key, and certificate level
options.C: Yes, but RBAC can only be applied at the vault level, not to individual secrets
options.D: Yes, but only with a Premium tier Key Vault SKU

questions.rationale: Azure RBAC is now the recommended access model for Key Vault, providing fine-grained control at vault, secret, key, and certificate levels. The legacy vault access policy model is still supported but not preferred.

optionRationales.A: Azure RBAC is fully supported for Key Vault and is the recommended model for new deployments.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Azure RBAC is recommended and supports data-plane control at vault, secret, key, and certificate granularity.
optionRationales.C: Azure RBAC for Key Vault works at the vault level and can be scoped to individual secrets, keys, or certificates.
optionRationales.D: Azure RBAC for Key Vault is available on both Standard and Premium SKUs with no tier restriction.

references.text: Azure RBAC vs vault access policy for Key Vault

questions.question: An application needs to read secrets from Key Vault but must not access keys or certificates. Which built-in role is most appropriate?

options.A: Key Vault Administrator
options.B: Key Vault Reader
options.C: Key Vault Secrets User
options.D: Key Vault Contributor

questions.rationale: Key Vault Secrets User grants read access (Get, List) specifically to secrets — it cannot access keys or certificates.

optionRationales.A: Key Vault Administrator has full control over all vault operations including keys, secrets, and certificates.
optionRationales.B: Key Vault Reader provides read access to vault metadata but does not grant access to secret values.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Key Vault Secrets User grants Get and List on secrets only — precisely what is required.
optionRationales.D: Key Vault Contributor is a management-plane role for vault configuration; it does not grant data-plane secret value access.

references.text: Azure Key Vault built-in RBAC roles

questions.question: A newly appointed Global Administrator needs to investigate a critical Azure resource permission problem but cannot see any Azure subscriptions. What must they do first?

options.A: Add themselves directly to the Owner role on each subscription
options.B: Go to Entra ID Properties → Manage access management for Azure resources → toggle Yes (Elevate access)
options.C: Create a PIM eligible assignment for Contributor at the management group level
options.D: Contact Microsoft Support to grant access

questions.rationale: Global Administrator does not automatically have Azure resource access. The 'Elevate access' toggle in Entra ID Properties grants User Access Administrator at root management group scope.

optionRationales.A: Directly adding to Owner roles requires already having permission to do so — a GA without elevation cannot perform this.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Elevate access in Entra ID Properties grants User Access Administrator at root scope, from which further Azure role assignments can be made.
optionRationales.C: Creating PIM eligible assignments also requires already having the permission to manage Azure RBAC.
optionRationales.D: Microsoft Support does not grant Azure resource access; this is a self-service action via the Entra portal.

references.text: Elevate access to manage all Azure subscriptions

questions.question: A security architect asks how long CAE-issued access tokens can remain valid for a user with no critical events. Which statement is correct?

options.A: CAE does not change token lifetimes; tokens still expire after 1 hour
options.B: CAE-issued tokens can be valid for up to 28 hours for CAE-capable resource providers
options.C: CAE tokens are permanent until a critical event occurs
options.D: CAE tokens expire after 8 hours regardless of events

questions.rationale: CAE extends access token lifetimes to up to 28 hours for CAE-capable resource providers (Exchange, SharePoint, Teams). If a critical event occurs, the token is revoked in near real-time (5-15 minutes).

optionRationales.A: CAE explicitly extends token lifetime beyond the default 1-hour window; 1-hour expiry is the non-CAE behavior.
optionRationales.B: Correct. CAE-issued tokens can be valid for up to 28 hours, with near-real-time revocation on critical events.
optionRationales.C: CAE tokens are not permanent — they still have a maximum lifetime of 28 hours and can be revoked by critical events.
optionRationales.D: The CAE maximum lifetime is 28 hours, not 8 hours.

questions.question: A developer registers an app and selects 'Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts.' Which user population can sign in?

options.A: Only users from the developer's own Entra tenant
options.B: Users from any Entra tenant plus personal Microsoft accounts (outlook.com, hotmail.com)
options.C: Only personal Microsoft accounts
options.D: Guest users from federated tenants only

questions.rationale: The multi-tenant + personal MSA account type allows both organizational accounts from any Entra tenant and personal Microsoft accounts.

optionRationales.A: Single-tenant restricts sign-in to the home tenant only; multi-tenant + MSA explicitly opens to all Entra tenants and personal accounts.
optionRationales.B: Correct. This account type covers all Entra tenants (any org directory) plus personal MSAs.
optionRationales.C: 'Personal MSAs only' is a separate, more restrictive account type that excludes organizational accounts.
optionRationales.D: Guest users from federated tenants are not a distinct account type selection; they would authenticate via their home tenant.

references.text: Supported account types in app registration

questions.question: A developer receives error AADSTS50011 after sign-in when the app redirects to its callback URL. What is the cause?

options.A: The user's MFA registration is incomplete
options.B: The redirect URI in the token request does not match any registered redirect URI on the app registration
options.C: The app's client secret has expired
options.D: The tenant has blocked third-party app registrations

questions.rationale: AADSTS50011 is the redirect URI mismatch error — the URI in the authorization request is not registered on the app. All redirect URIs must be HTTPS (except localhost) and must exactly match a registered value.

optionRationales.A: Incomplete MFA registration produces a different error during the sign-in challenge, not AADSTS50011.
optionRationales.B: Correct. AADSTS50011 specifically indicates the redirect URI in the request does not match any registered URI.
optionRationales.C: An expired client secret would fail token acquisition, not redirect URI validation.
optionRationales.D: Blocked app registrations would prevent the app from existing, not produce a redirect URI error.

questions.question: A platform team manages 25 app registrations and wants to add GitHub Actions federated credentials to each. How many federated identity credentials does each app registration support?

options.A: 100 per app registration
options.B: 20 per app registration
options.C: 5 per app registration
options.D: Unlimited

questions.rationale: Each app registration (and each user-assigned managed identity) supports a maximum of 20 federated identity credentials.

optionRationales.A: 100 is the limit for custom directory roles per tenant, not federated credentials per app.
optionRationales.B: Correct. The hard limit is 20 federated identity credentials per app registration or user-assigned managed identity.
optionRationales.C: 5 is far below the actual limit; 20 is the correct maximum.
optionRationales.D: There is a hard cap of 20; federated credentials are not unlimited.

questions.question: An app has been granted the delegated permission User.Read.All. A signed-in user with read access only to their own profile uses the app. What user data can the app actually read?

options.A: All users in the tenant because User.Read.All was granted
options.B: Only the signed-in user's own profile — effective rights are the intersection of the granted permission and the user's own privileges
options.C: All users if admin consent was granted, or only the current user otherwise
options.D: No users — delegated User.Read.All requires the user to also be a Global Reader

questions.rationale: Delegated permissions operate on behalf of the signed-in user. Effective access is the intersection of what the permission grants and what the user themselves can access.

optionRationales.A: The permission scope alone does not override the user's own access rights; intersection applies.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Effective rights = intersection of the delegated permission and the signed-in user's own privileges.
optionRationales.C: Admin consent enables the app to call the API, but the effective data access is still limited to the user's own rights.
optionRationales.D: Global Reader is not required; the user simply accesses only what their own account can access.

questions.question: A daemon application uses the client credentials flow. In which token claim will the granted application permissions appear?

options.A: scp (scope) claim
options.B: roles claim
options.C: groups claim
options.D: wids claim

questions.rationale: Application permissions appear in the roles claim. Delegated permissions appear in the scp claim. groups holds group memberships. wids holds directory role template IDs.

optionRationales.A: The scp claim carries delegated permission scopes for user-context (delegated) flows, not client credentials.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Application permissions granted via admin consent appear in the roles claim of client credentials tokens.
optionRationales.C: The groups claim contains the user's or service principal's group memberships, not API permissions.
optionRationales.D: The wids claim contains directory role template IDs assigned to the user, not API permissions.

references.text: Roles and scopes in access tokens

questions.question: An IT admin needs to pre-consent to an application on behalf of all users in the tenant. Which URL format should they navigate to?

options.A: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id={appId}&response_type=code
options.B: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/adminconsent?client_id={appId}
options.C: https://portal.azure.com/#blade/adminconsent?appId={appId}
options.D: https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/{id}/appRoleAssignments

questions.rationale: The admin consent URL is https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/adminconsent?client_id={appId}. Navigating this URL while signed in as an eligible admin grants tenant-wide consent.

optionRationales.A: This is the standard authorization endpoint for user-delegated sign-in — not the admin consent endpoint.
optionRationales.B: Correct. The /adminconsent endpoint grants tenant-wide consent for all permissions the app requests.
optionRationales.C: This is not a valid Azure portal URL for admin consent.
optionRationales.D: This is a Microsoft Graph API endpoint for programmatic app role assignment, not an admin consent URL.

references.text: Construct the URL for admin consent

questions.question: An Application Administrator wants to grant admin consent for an app requesting the Microsoft Graph application permission User.Read.All. Can they?

options.A: Yes — Application Administrator can grant all admin consent including Microsoft Graph application permissions
options.B: No — Application Administrator cannot grant admin consent for Microsoft Graph application permissions; only Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator can
options.C: Yes, but only if they are also the owner of the app registration
options.D: No — only Cloud Application Administrator can grant Microsoft Graph application permissions

questions.rationale: Application Administrator can grant admin consent for most permissions but is explicitly excluded from Microsoft Graph application permissions. Global Administrator and Privileged Role Administrator do not have this restriction.

optionRationales.A: Application Administrator has an explicit exclusion for Microsoft Graph application permissions — they cannot grant these.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Microsoft Graph application permissions require Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator consent.
optionRationales.C: App ownership does not extend the Application Administrator's consent authority for Graph application permissions.
optionRationales.D: Cloud Application Administrator has the same consent limitation as Application Administrator for Graph application permissions.

references.text: Grant tenant-wide admin consent to an application

questions.question: A developer wants to implement custom in-app authorization with 'Admin' and 'Viewer' roles surfaced in the access token. What should they define?

options.A: OAuth2PermissionScopes (exposed API scopes) on the Expose an API blade
options.B: App roles on the App roles blade of the app registration, assigned to users, groups, or service principals
options.C: Conditional Access authentication contexts
options.D: Custom directory roles using action strings

questions.rationale: App roles are for custom RBAC inside the application, defined on the App roles blade and emitted in the roles claim. OAuth2PermissionScopes define what delegated permissions other apps can request from your API.

optionRationales.A: OAuth2PermissionScopes (Expose an API) define delegated permissions that other apps can request — not in-app role authorization.
optionRationales.B: Correct. App roles are purpose-built for in-app RBAC, appear in the roles claim, and can be assigned to users, groups, or SPs.
optionRationales.C: Authentication contexts are CA constructs for step-up authentication, not application authorization roles.
optionRationales.D: Custom directory roles govern Entra ID directory operations, not in-app authorization logic.

references.text: Add app roles in your application
Scopes and permissions in the Microsoft identity platform

questions.question: Which set of permissions is classified as 'low-impact' by default, allowing user consent when the recommended balanced consent policy is active?

options.A: User.ReadWrite.All, Mail.Read, Files.Read.All
options.B: openid, profile, email, offline_access, User.Read
options.C: Calendars.ReadWrite, Contacts.Read, Sites.Read.All
options.D: Group.Read.All, Directory.Read.All

questions.rationale: The low-impact classification includes openid, profile, email, offline_access, and User.Read — the minimal permissions for basic sign-in. All others require admin review.

optionRationales.A: User.ReadWrite.All, Mail.Read, and Files.Read.All are high-impact permissions requiring admin consent.
optionRationales.B: Correct. The five low-impact defaults (openid, profile, email, offline_access, User.Read) cover basic identity and sign-in only.
optionRationales.C: Calendars.ReadWrite, Contacts.Read, and Sites.Read.All access user data and require admin review.
optionRationales.D: Group.Read.All and Directory.Read.All expose organizational directory data and require admin consent.

questions.question: A developer is creating a new client secret for a production application. What is the maximum duration they can configure?

options.A: 6 months
options.B: 12 months
options.C: 24 months
options.D: Client secrets do not expire by default

questions.rationale: Client secrets can be configured for up to 24 months. Microsoft recommends certificates or federated credentials for production — secrets must be manually rotated.

optionRationales.A: 6 months is a valid duration but is not the maximum; 24 months is the upper limit.
optionRationales.B: 12 months is a valid option but not the maximum allowed.
optionRationales.C: Correct. 24 months is the maximum client secret lifetime. Microsoft recommends certificates or WIF for production instead.
optionRationales.D: All client secrets have an expiry; they do not remain valid indefinitely.

questions.question: When configuring a federated identity credential on an app registration to trust GitHub Actions OIDC tokens, what must be set as the audience?

options.A: https://github.com/{org}/{repo}
options.B: api://AzureADTokenExchange
options.C: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}
options.D: urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer

questions.rationale: The audience for all federated identity credentials is always api://AzureADTokenExchange, regardless of the external OIDC provider (GitHub, GCP, Kubernetes, Azure DevOps).

optionRationales.A: The GitHub repo URL is used in the subject claim to scope the trust to a specific repo/branch — not in the audience field.
optionRationales.B: Correct. api://AzureADTokenExchange is the fixed audience value for all WIF federated identity credentials.
optionRationales.C: The tenant login URL is the token issuer endpoint, not the audience value for WIF.
optionRationales.D: This URN is the grant type for JWT bearer assertion flows, not the WIF audience value.

questions.question: Users want the My Apps portal organized by department so each team sees only their relevant apps. What feature and license requirement enables this?

options.A: App Proxy connector groups — no additional license required
options.B: Collections in the My Apps portal — requires P1 license
options.C: Administrative Units scoped to each department — requires P1
options.D: Dynamic group assignment to each app — requires P1

questions.rationale: Collections in the My Apps portal (https://myapps.microsoft.com) allow admins to group apps by department or role. Collections require P1.

optionRationales.A: App Proxy connector groups are backend publishing infrastructure; they do not affect the My Apps portal user experience.
optionRationales.B: Correct. My Apps Collections group tiles by any logical category and require a P1 license.
optionRationales.C: Administrative Units scope directory administration, not app visibility in the My Apps portal.
optionRationales.D: Dynamic groups can be used for app assignment but do not provide the visual collection/grouping feature in My Apps.

questions.question: An admin has configured a single Application Proxy connector for an intranet site and is concerned about availability. What is the minimum recommended configuration for high availability?

options.A: One connector is sufficient — the service is managed by Microsoft in the cloud
options.B: At least 2 connectors in a connector group
options.C: At least 3 globally distributed connectors
options.D: Connectors must be on the same subnet as the published application

questions.rationale: Microsoft recommends at least 2 connectors per connector group for high availability. The cloud endpoint is Microsoft-managed but the on-prem connector agents need local redundancy.

optionRationales.A: The cloud component is managed by Microsoft, but the on-prem connector agents can fail and require local redundancy.
optionRationales.B: Correct. A minimum of 2 connectors per connector group is the Microsoft HA recommendation.
optionRationales.C: Global distribution is optional for performance; the HA minimum is 2 connectors, not 3.
optionRationales.D: Connectors only require outbound HTTPS (443) access; they do not need to be co-located with the application.

questions.question: An app is configured with Linked SSO. An admin tries to apply a CA policy requiring MFA to it. Will the CA policy be enforced?

options.A: Yes — CA is enforced regardless of SSO mode
options.B: No — Linked SSO does not issue an Entra token, so CA cannot intercept or enforce anything
options.C: Yes — but only if the app is also included in All cloud apps
options.D: No — CA can only target apps using SAML or OIDC SSO modes

questions.rationale: Linked SSO is simply a launch tile that redirects users to an external URL. No Entra token is issued, so there is no Entra session for CA to enforce.

optionRationales.A: CA requires an Entra token to be issued; Linked SSO produces no token and therefore cannot be targeted.
optionRationales.B: Correct. No Entra token = no CA enforcement. Linked SSO bypasses the entire Entra authentication flow.
optionRationales.C: Including the app in All cloud apps does not help if the SSO mode produces no Entra token.
optionRationales.D: Password-based and header-based SSO also issue Entra tokens and can be targeted by CA; the issue is specific to Linked SSO.

references.text: Linked SSO mode for enterprise apps

questions.question: A security analyst opens the Sign-in logs blade in the Entra admin center. Which four tabs are available?

options.A: Users, Guests, Service accounts, Managed services
options.B: Interactive user sign-ins, Non-interactive user sign-ins, Service principal sign-ins, Managed identity sign-ins
options.C: Successful, Failed, Interrupted, Risky
options.D: MFA, SSPR, Privileged, Guest

questions.rationale: The Sign-in logs blade has four tabs: Interactive user, Non-interactive user (token refresh, silent auth), Service principal, and Managed identity sign-ins.

optionRationales.A: The tabs are organized by identity type and interaction type, not by user classification.
optionRationales.B: Correct. The four tabs are Interactive, Non-interactive, Service principal, and Managed identity — each a separate log stream.
optionRationales.C: Success/failure filtering is done within the log tabs, not as separate tabs.
optionRationales.D: MFA, SSPR, PIM, and guest activity are not separate sign-in log tabs.

questions.question: A company on Microsoft Entra ID Free needs to investigate a sign-in event from 45 days ago. Will the logs be available in the portal?

options.A: Yes — sign-in logs are retained for 90 days across all tiers
options.B: No — Free tier retains sign-in logs for 7 days; P1/P2 extends this to 30 days; longer retention requires exporting to Log Analytics or Storage
options.C: Yes — sign-in logs are retained for 180 days
options.D: No — Free tier does not include any sign-in logging

questions.rationale: Free tier retains sign-in logs for 7 days. P1/P2 extends this to 30 days. For retention beyond 30 days, export via Diagnostic Settings to Log Analytics or Storage Account is required.

optionRationales.A: 90-day retention is not available in any tier natively; maximum portal retention is 30 days with P1/P2.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Free=7 days, P1/P2=30 days, and longer retention requires Diagnostic Settings export.
optionRationales.C: 180-day retention is not offered natively; this requires a Log Analytics or Storage Account destination.
optionRationales.D: Free tier does include sign-in logging; the limitation is retention duration (7 days), not existence.

references.text: Microsoft Entra log retention by tier

questions.question: A security team wants to detect when any user in the tenant granted OAuth consent to a third-party app in the past 30 days. Which log and category should they query?

options.A: Sign-in logs → filter by application name
options.B: Audit logs → Category: ApplicationManagement → Activity: Consent to application
options.C: Provisioning logs → filter by action type
options.D: Identity Protection → Risky sign-ins → filter by app

questions.rationale: OAuth consent grants are recorded as audit events under the ApplicationManagement category with the 'Consent to application' activity — the authoritative source for illicit consent grant investigation.

optionRationales.A: Sign-in logs record authentication events, not OAuth consent grant events.
optionRationales.B: Correct. ApplicationManagement audit logs capture every consent grant including who consented and which permissions were granted.
optionRationales.C: Provisioning logs track SCIM-based attribute synchronization, not OAuth consent.
optionRationales.D: Identity Protection risky sign-ins tracks authentication risk, not consent activity.

questions.question: A company wants to give B2B guest users from a partner organization access to an on-prem web app published via Application Proxy, with MFA enforced. Is this supported?

options.A: No — Application Proxy only supports member users in the home tenant
options.B: Yes — Application Proxy supports B2B guest users with pre-authentication set to Microsoft Entra ID, and CA/MFA is enforced during pre-auth
options.C: Yes, but only with Passthrough pre-authentication
options.D: No — B2B guests must use GSA Private Access instead of App Proxy

questions.rationale: Application Proxy fully supports B2B guest users when pre-authentication is set to Microsoft Entra ID. During pre-auth, CA/MFA policies are enforced against the guest's home-tenant credentials.

optionRationales.A: B2B guest user support is a documented App Proxy capability when using Entra ID pre-authentication.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Entra ID pre-authentication enforces CA/MFA for B2B guests via cross-tenant trust before forwarding to the backend.
optionRationales.C: Passthrough pre-authentication skips Entra auth entirely, providing no MFA enforcement for anyone including guests.
optionRationales.D: App Proxy and GSA Private Access can both serve B2B guests; they are not mutually exclusive.

questions.question: A developer's app requests group claims in the token. The signed-in user is a member of 210 Entra ID groups. What happens?

options.A: All 210 groups are included in the token
options.B: A groups overage claim is added and the app must call Microsoft Graph to retrieve the full group list
options.C: The token request is rejected due to the group count
options.D: The token includes the first 150 groups alphabetically

questions.rationale: When a user belongs to more than 150 groups, Entra cannot include all groups in the token due to size limits. A groups overage claim is added and the app must call Graph for the complete list.

optionRationales.A: The token size limit prevents including all groups when membership exceeds 150; an overage claim is used instead.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Groups overage claim signals the app to call Graph API for the full membership list.
optionRationales.C: The token request is not rejected; the overage mechanism gracefully handles large group memberships.
optionRationales.D: Groups are not truncated alphabetically; the overage claim mechanism is used for the entire list.

questions.question: An app registration needs to be temporarily disabled without losing its configuration, credentials, or consent grants. What is the correct action?

options.A: Delete the app registration and recreate it later
options.B: Remove all user assignments from the enterprise application
options.C: Deactivate the application (set accountEnabled = false on the service principal)
options.D: Revoke all credentials and disable the CA policy targeting it

questions.rationale: Deactivating an application disables the service principal's sign-in (accountEnabled = false) without deleting the app registration, credentials, consent grants, or service principal.

optionRationales.A: Deleting the app registration removes the home-tenant service principal, all credentials, and all consent grants permanently.
optionRationales.B: Removing user assignments only affects which users see the app in My Apps; it does not block sign-ins from the app itself.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Setting accountEnabled = false on the service principal blocks all sign-ins while preserving full configuration.
optionRationales.D: Revoking credentials breaks active integrations and disabling a CA policy does not block the app from issuing tokens.

references.text: Disable how a user signs in for a third-party application

questions.question: Which resource types can be bundled inside an entitlement management access package?

options.A: Azure virtual machines, storage accounts, and Key Vaults
options.B: Security groups, Microsoft 365 groups, enterprise applications, and SharePoint Online sites
options.C: Microsoft Entra roles, Azure resource roles, and custom application roles only
options.D: Exchange Online mailboxes, Teams channels, and Yammer communities

questions.rationale: Access packages can bundle security groups, M365 groups, enterprise applications (including app roles), and SharePoint Online sites.

optionRationales.A: Azure resources (VMs, storage, Key Vaults) are managed via Azure RBAC and PIM for Azure resources, not entitlement management.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Security groups, M365 groups, enterprise apps, and SPO sites are the four supported access package resource types.
optionRationales.C: Entra and Azure roles are managed through PIM directly; they are not bundled as resources in access packages.
optionRationales.D: Exchange mailboxes, Teams channels, and Yammer communities are not directly configurable as access package resources.

questions.question: A business unit owner should create and manage access packages within their catalog but not create new catalogs or manage resources in other catalogs. Which role is most appropriate?

options.A: Identity Governance Administrator (tenant-wide)
options.B: Catalog Owner of their specific catalog
options.C: Access Package Manager of their specific catalog
options.D: Access Package Assignment Manager of their specific catalog

questions.rationale: Access Package Manager can create and manage access packages within a specific catalog without catalog-level resource management or cross-catalog access.

optionRationales.A: Identity Governance Administrator is a tenant-wide role — far broader than managing one catalog's packages.
optionRationales.B: Catalog Owner can manage resources in the catalog and delegate roles to others — broader than needed.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Access Package Manager is scoped to creating and managing packages within the specific catalog only.
optionRationales.D: Access Package Assignment Manager can only manage assignments on existing packages, not create new packages.

references.text: Delegate catalog administration in entitlement management

questions.question: Which types of external identities can be represented as a connected organization in entitlement management?

options.A: Only other Microsoft Entra tenants with B2B collaboration enabled
options.B: Microsoft Entra tenants, SAML/WS-Fed identity providers, verified email domains (email OTP), and Microsoft Account domains
options.C: Only Microsoft Entra tenants and federated SAML providers
options.D: Any identity provider with an OIDC endpoint

questions.rationale: Connected organizations support four types: Entra tenants, SAML/WS-Fed IdPs, verified email domains (email OTP for non-Entra users), and Microsoft Account domains.

optionRationales.A: Connected organizations also support SAML/WS-Fed IdPs, email OTP domains, and MSA domains — not Entra tenants only.
optionRationales.B: Correct. All four types (Entra tenant, SAML/WS-Fed, email domain/OTP, MSA) are supported as connected organizations.
optionRationales.C: Email OTP domains and MSA domains are also supported connected organization types.
optionRationales.D: OIDC is not listed as a distinct connected organization type in entitlement management.

questions.question: When an external user's last access package assignment expires, what is the default lifecycle behavior in entitlement management?

options.A: The guest account is deleted immediately
options.B: The guest account is blocked from sign-in for 30 days, then deleted
options.C: The guest account remains active indefinitely with no automatic cleanup
options.D: The guest account is converted to a member account

questions.rationale: The default guest lifecycle in entitlement management blocks the external user for 30 days after their last assignment ends, then deletes the account. This grace period is configurable.

optionRationales.A: Immediate deletion is not the default; the 30-day sign-in block grace period is designed to prevent accidental data loss.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Default: 30-day sign-in block followed by account deletion after the last assignment expires.
optionRationales.C: Entitlement management includes automatic guest lifecycle management; accounts do not persist indefinitely by default.
optionRationales.D: External users are not automatically converted to members; they are removed when assignments end.

references.text: Access reviews on entitlement management access packages

questions.question: A governance admin wants to add the Exchange Administrator role to an access package for time-limited admin access. How must the role assignment type be configured in the package?

options.A: Active assignment — users receive active rights immediately upon package assignment
options.B: Eligible assignment only — privileged Entra roles in access packages must always be eligible, never active
options.C: Permanent active assignment — roles in access packages are always permanent
options.D: Privileged roles cannot be added to access packages at all

questions.rationale: When Microsoft Entra privileged roles are added to an access package, they must be Eligible assignments. This integrates with PIM so users still must activate the role JIT.

optionRationales.A: Active assignments for privileged roles in access packages would create standing admin rights — explicitly not allowed.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Privileged Entra roles in access packages must be Eligible, preserving the JIT activation requirement via PIM.
optionRationales.C: Permanent active assignments for privileged roles in access packages are not permitted.
optionRationales.D: Privileged roles can be added to access packages but must be configured as Eligible assignments only.

references.text: Entitlement management — assign roles via access packages

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid reviewer type for an access review?

options.A: Group owner
options.B: Manager with a configurable fallback for users without a manager
options.C: Automated AI reviewer based on usage patterns
options.D: Self-review (the user themselves)

questions.rationale: Valid reviewer types are: group owner, selected users/groups, self-review, manager with fallback, and multi-stage combinations. There is no AI reviewer type. Recommendations are decision aids for human reviewers.

optionRationales.A: Group owner is a valid and commonly used reviewer type for group membership reviews.
optionRationales.B: Manager with fallback is a valid reviewer type; fallback handles cases where no manager is set.
optionRationales.C: Correct. There is no automated AI reviewer type. System recommendations guide human reviewers but do not make decisions.
optionRationales.D: Self-review is a valid reviewer type, useful for attestation scenarios where users confirm their own need for access.

references.text: Create an access review of groups and applications

questions.topic: Access reviews — auto-apply on synced groups
questions.question: An access review is configured with auto-apply to remove denied users from a security group synced from on-premises AD via Entra Connect. What happens when a user is denied?

options.A: The user is automatically removed from the cloud group and the change is written back to AD DS
options.B: The user is removed from the cloud group only if the group was originally created in Entra ID
options.C: Auto-apply does not remove members from groups synced from on-premises AD; removals must be made in the on-prem directory
options.D: The user is flagged for removal and deleted at the next Connect sync cycle

questions.rationale: Access review auto-apply removes members from cloud-mastered groups. Groups synced from on-prem AD are read-only in the cloud; membership changes must be made in the on-prem directory.

optionRationales.A: There is no writeback path for access review decisions to AD DS; on-prem groups must be managed on-prem.
optionRationales.B: Auto-apply works for groups created in (and mastered in) Entra ID — not for synced groups regardless of origin.
optionRationales.C: Correct. On-prem synced groups are read-only from the cloud; auto-apply cannot modify their membership.
optionRationales.D: Connect sync pushes on-prem changes to the cloud; it does not execute cloud access review decisions in reverse.

questions.question: Which license is required to run an access review targeting users inactive for a configurable period, with system-generated denial recommendations for inactive accounts?

options.A: Microsoft Entra ID P1
options.B: Microsoft Entra ID P2
options.C: Microsoft Entra ID Governance
options.D: Microsoft Defender for Identity

questions.rationale: Access reviews with inactive user targeting and automated recommendations require Microsoft Entra ID Governance. Basic access reviews require P2; this advanced capability requires the Governance step-up.

optionRationales.A: P1 does not include access reviews at all; P2 is the minimum for basic reviews.
optionRationales.B: P2 covers basic access reviews but not the inactive-user targeting with recommendations — that requires Governance.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Inactive-user reviews with recommendations are a Governance-tier feature, not included in P2 alone.
optionRationales.D: Defender for Identity focuses on on-prem AD threat detection; it has no role in access review licensing.

references.text: Inactive user recommendations in access reviews

questions.question: What is the difference between a Permanent Eligible and a Time-bound Eligible assignment in PIM?

options.A: Permanent Eligible assignments grant active access; time-bound require activation
options.B: Both are eligible assignments requiring JIT activation; permanent has no expiry while time-bound is valid only for a configured date range
options.C: Permanent Eligible assignments bypass MFA on activation; time-bound do not
options.D: There is no difference — all eligible assignments are permanent by default

questions.rationale: Both Permanent and Time-bound Eligible assignments require JIT activation with configured requirements (MFA, justification, approval). The only difference is the assignment duration.

optionRationales.A: Permanent Eligible does not grant active access; it still requires JIT activation — the 'permanent' refers to no expiry on the eligibility.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Both types require activation; permanent = no expiry on eligibility, time-bound = eligibility valid within a date range.
optionRationales.C: MFA requirements at activation are configured in role settings and apply equally to both permanent and time-bound eligible assignments.
optionRationales.D: Eligible assignments can be either permanent (no expiry) or time-bound (with an end date) — these are distinct configurations.

references.text: Eligible vs permanent assignments in PIM

questions.question: A team wants to give a service principal JIT eligible access to the Contributor role on a subscription via PIM. Is this supported?

options.A: Yes — service principals can be given eligible assignments just like users
options.B: No — service principals can only receive time-limited Active assignments in PIM, not Eligible assignments
options.C: No — service principals cannot be assigned any Azure roles via PIM
options.D: Yes, but only through PIM for Groups, not direct role assignment

questions.rationale: Service principals cannot be given Eligible assignments in PIM because they cannot interactively activate. The supported pattern is time-limited Active assignments for bounded, non-JIT access.

optionRationales.A: Service principals cannot perform the interactive activation steps required for eligible assignments.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Time-limited Active assignments are the supported PIM pattern for service principals.
optionRationales.C: Service principals can receive Azure role assignments via PIM — they just cannot use the Eligible assignment type.
optionRationales.D: PIM for Groups works with user eligible membership; it does not enable eligible assignments for service principals on Azure roles.

references.text: PIM for service principals (workload identities) and groups

questions.question: A user is given Eligible Membership in a role-assignable security group via PIM for Groups. The group is assigned the User Administrator role. What must the user do to gain User Administrator rights?

options.A: Nothing — eligible group membership automatically grants the role
options.B: Activate their group membership in PIM; upon activation they become an active member and the User Administrator role is granted
options.C: Request admin approval for the role and the group separately
options.D: The user must be made directly eligible for User Administrator — PIM for Groups cannot cascade roles

questions.rationale: PIM for Groups makes a user eligible for group membership. When they activate, they become an active member and inherit the group's role assignments, including User Administrator.

optionRationales.A: Eligible membership still requires activation; eligibility alone does not grant active group membership or the role.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Activating eligible group membership grants active membership, which cascades to the group's role assignments.
optionRationales.C: A single activation of group membership is all that is required; there is no separate role approval step.
optionRationales.D: PIM for Groups with a role-assignable group is the supported pattern for wrapping multiple roles in one PIM activation.

questions.question: A new security team wants to quickly identify all users with permanent active admin role assignments and convert them to eligible JIT assignments without searching each role manually. Which PIM feature helps?

options.A: PIM security alerts configuration
options.B: PIM Discovery and Insights (formerly Security Wizard)
options.C: Entra ID Identity Secure Score recommendations
options.D: Identity Protection risky users report

questions.rationale: PIM Discovery and Insights provides a single-pane view that scans all Entra role assignments, highlights permanent active admins, and allows bulk conversion to eligible assignments.

optionRationales.A: PIM security alerts fire on threshold violations; they do not provide a bulk discovery and conversion workflow.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Discovery and Insights scans all role assignments and provides one-click conversion to eligible.
optionRationales.C: Identity Secure Score offers posture recommendations but does not provide PIM-specific bulk conversion tooling.
optionRationales.D: The risky users report tracks identity compromise risk, not admin role assignment standing-privilege issues.

references.text: PIM Discovery and Insights for Microsoft Entra roles

questions.question: A compliance team needs PIM activation and assignment audit logs for the past 6 months. The PIM portal only shows 30 days. How do they get the full 6 months?

options.A: The full history is in the Entra audit logs blade without additional configuration
options.B: PIM audit history is limited to 30 days in the portal; longer retention requires exporting to Log Analytics via Diagnostic Settings
options.C: The history is in the Microsoft 365 Compliance Center under Audit search
options.D: PIM audit logs are retained for 90 days; the 6-month data does not exist

questions.rationale: The PIM portal retains audit history for 30 days. For longer retention, Diagnostic Settings must stream logs to a Log Analytics workspace or Storage Account.

optionRationales.A: The Entra audit logs blade also has a 30-day portal retention limit; longer retention requires Diagnostic Settings export.
optionRationales.B: Correct. 30-day portal limit for PIM audit history; Diagnostic Settings to Log Analytics or Storage enables longer retention.
optionRationales.C: Microsoft Purview Compliance Center captures Microsoft 365 service audit events, not Entra ID PIM activation logs.
optionRationales.D: PIM portal retention is 30 days (not 90); 6-month data is available only if Diagnostic Settings were configured in advance.

questions.question: A workflow must immediately disable a user account and remove all group memberships the moment their termination date attribute is set in the HR system. Which Lifecycle Workflow category is correct?

options.A: Joiner — Onboard new hire
options.B: Mover — Real-time employee change
options.C: Leaver — Real-time termination
options.D: Leaver — Pre-offboard employee

questions.rationale: The Real-time termination leaver template triggers immediately when a configured attribute (e.g., employeeLeaveDateTime) is set, executing tasks like disable account and remove groups in real time.

optionRationales.A: Joiner workflows handle new hire onboarding; this is a termination (leaver) scenario.
optionRationales.B: Mover handles role or department changes for existing employees, not terminations.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Real-time leaver triggers immediately on attribute change — the correct match for instant termination processing.
optionRationales.D: Pre-offboard runs before the leave date on a schedule — it does not trigger in real time when the attribute is set.

references.text: Lifecycle Workflows — joiner mover leaver categories

questions.question: How many Lifecycle Workflows can be created per Entra tenant?

options.D: Unlimited

questions.rationale: The current limit is 100 Lifecycle Workflows per tenant. Organizations needing more complex automation can use custom task extensions (Logic App) to trigger external orchestration.

optionRationales.A: 10 is far below the actual limit of 100 workflows per tenant.
optionRationales.B: 50 is below the actual limit; the correct limit is 100.
optionRationales.C: Correct. 100 Lifecycle Workflows per tenant is the documented limit.
optionRationales.D: Lifecycle Workflows are not unlimited; the hard cap is 100 per tenant.

references.text: Lifecycle Workflows — tenant limits and considerations

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid trigger type for a Lifecycle Workflow?

options.A: Scheduled (e.g., 7 days before employeeHireDate)
options.B: On-demand (manually triggered for a specific user)
options.C: Real-time (triggered by an HR system attribute change)
options.D: Policy-triggered (triggered by a Conditional Access policy evaluation)

questions.rationale: Lifecycle Workflow triggers are: Scheduled (attribute-relative or every 3 hours), On-demand, Real-time (mover/leaver), and Group membership change. CA policy evaluations do not trigger workflows.

optionRationales.A: Scheduled is a valid trigger type, supporting attribute-relative timing like '7 days before employeeHireDate'.
optionRationales.B: On-demand is a valid trigger type for manual execution against a specific user.
optionRationales.C: Real-time is a valid trigger type for mover and leaver workflows triggered by HR attribute changes.
optionRationales.D: Correct. Conditional Access policy evaluations are a separate system and do not trigger Lifecycle Workflows.

questions.question: Which of the following is a built-in Lifecycle Workflow task that does NOT require a custom task extension?

options.A: Provision a user account in ServiceNow
options.B: Send a welcome email to the new hire
options.C: Create a new mailbox in Exchange on-premises
options.D: Provision a virtual machine in Azure

questions.rationale: Built-in tasks include: Send welcome email, Generate TAP, Add to groups/Teams, Enable/Disable account, Assign manager, Remove licenses, Delete user, Send manager email. External system provisioning requires custom task extensions (Logic App).

optionRationales.A: ServiceNow provisioning requires a custom task extension connecting to an external Logic App or API.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Send welcome email is a native built-in LCW task requiring no custom extension.
optionRationales.C: Exchange on-premises mailbox creation requires a custom task extension; it is not a built-in task.
optionRationales.D: Azure VM provisioning requires a custom task extension or separate automation outside LCW.

questions.question: A governance team needs to send Entra sign-in logs simultaneously to Log Analytics (for KQL), a Storage Account (7-year archive), and an Event Hub (for their SIEM). How many diagnostic settings can be configured per Entra resource?

options.A: 1 — only one destination can be configured
options.B: 3 — one per destination type (Log Analytics, Storage, Event Hub)
options.C: 5 — up to 5 diagnostic settings can be configured per resource
options.D: Unlimited

questions.rationale: Microsoft Entra ID supports up to 5 diagnostic settings per resource, allowing simultaneous fan-out to multiple destinations.

optionRationales.A: Multiple diagnostic settings are supported; only one destination is not the limit.
optionRationales.B: The limit is 5 diagnostic settings per resource, not one per destination type.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Up to 5 diagnostic settings per Entra resource enables fan-out to multiple simultaneous destinations.
optionRationales.D: The limit is 5 diagnostic settings per resource; they are not unlimited.

questions.question: Which retention schedule applies to both sign-in logs and audit logs in the Entra admin center portal?

options.A: Both retain for 90 days on all tiers
options.B: Sign-in logs: 30 days P1; Audit logs: 90 days P1
options.C: Both retain for 7 days on Free tier and 30 days on P1/P2
options.D: Audit logs are retained indefinitely; sign-in logs follow the 7/30-day schedule

questions.rationale: Both sign-in logs and audit logs follow the same schedule: 7 days on Free, 30 days on P1/P2. For longer retention of either type, configure Diagnostic Settings.

optionRationales.A: 90-day native portal retention is not offered for any log type at any tier.
optionRationales.B: Audit logs follow the same 7/30-day schedule as sign-in logs; they are not retained for 90 days.
optionRationales.C: Correct. Both log types: Free=7 days, P1/P2=30 days; Diagnostic Settings required for longer retention.
optionRationales.D: Audit logs are not retained indefinitely; they follow the same 7/30-day portal retention as sign-in logs.
