questions.question: An Entra administrator wants a numeric metric showing the organization's identity security posture and a prioritized list of recommended actions. Which feature provides this?

options.A: Microsoft Secure Score in Microsoft Defender XDR
options.B: Identity Protection risk summary dashboard
options.C: Microsoft Entra Identity Secure Score
options.D: Compliance Manager in Microsoft Purview

questions.rationale: Microsoft Entra Identity Secure Score provides a numeric score measuring identity security posture against Microsoft best practices with prioritized improvement actions (block legacy auth, require MFA, enable SSPR, etc.).

optionRationales.A: Microsoft Secure Score in Defender XDR covers a broader multi-workload security posture, not identity-specific posture alone.
optionRationales.B: Identity Protection risk summary tracks active risk detections and risky users; it does not provide a posture score or improvement actions.

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.topic: Microsoft Entra ID licensing — P1 vs P2 vs Governance
questions.question: An organization wants Conditional Access, dynamic group membership, password writeback, AND Privileged Identity Management plus risk-based Conditional Access. Which combination of Microsoft Entra ID licenses is minimum-required for ALL of these scenarios?

options.A: Microsoft Entra ID Free for all users — these are baseline features.
options.B: Microsoft Entra ID P1 for every user covered by Conditional Access and dynamic groups; Microsoft Entra ID P2 (or P2-equivalent step-up) for the subset who use Privileged Identity Management and risk-based Conditional Access (Identity Protection).
options.C: Microsoft Entra ID Governance license for every user — it includes everything.
options.D: Microsoft 365 E3 covers every Identity feature.

questions.rationale: P1 covers Conditional Access, dynamic groups, password writeback, group-based access management, and SSPR. P2 adds Identity Protection (sign-in / user risk) and PIM. Microsoft Entra ID Governance is a separate step-up SKU (on top of P1/P2) for entitlement management advanced features, Lifecycle Workflows, sponsor approvers, and multi-stage access reviews. Microsoft 365 E3 includes Entra ID P1; E5 includes P2.

optionRationales.A: Entra ID Free doesn't include Conditional Access or dynamic groups.
optionRationales.B: Correct. P1 covers CA + dynamic groups + password writeback; P2 adds PIM and risk-based CA.
optionRationales.C: Governance is for governance step-up features — Conditional Access and PIM still need P1/P2.
optionRationales.D: M365 E3 includes P1 only; PIM and risk-based CA require P2 (in E5).

references.text: Microsoft Entra ID Free, P1, P2 feature comparison

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.topic: Microsoft Entra Verified ID — issuer/verifier roles
questions.question: An employer wants to issue verifiable credentials representing employment status that other organizations can later verify without contacting the issuer for each verification. Which Microsoft Entra feature is appropriate, and what two roles does it define?

options.A: Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration — defines guest and host roles.
options.B: Microsoft Entra Verified ID — based on open W3C standards (DIDs and verifiable credentials). The organization acts as ISSUER (cryptographically signs and presents a credential to the user, who stores it in a wallet) and any other org acts as VERIFIER (validates the credential and the user's possession without re-contacting the issuer).
options.C: Microsoft Entra Permissions Management — defines administrator and user roles.
options.D: Microsoft Entra Connect — defines source and target.

questions.rationale: Verified ID is Microsoft's implementation of decentralized identity (W3C verifiable credentials + DIDs). It defines the issuer/verifier pattern: an issuer cryptographically signs claims and gives them to the holder; a verifier later checks the cryptographic signature and the holder's presentation proof without re-contacting the issuer. Common SC-300 scenarios include employment, certifications, and access onboarding.

optionRationales.A: B2B collaboration is about cross-tenant identity sharing, not credential issuance.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Verified ID's issuer/verifier model is the documented architecture.
optionRationales.C: Permissions Management is CIEM, unrelated to credential issuance.
optionRationales.D: Connect is a sync product, unrelated.

references.text: Get started as an issuer of Verified ID

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.question: A security architect wants to assign a fine-grained 'EngineeringTeam = Cryptography' attribute to a subset of users so that Conditional Access policies, application filters, and PIM eligibility can target them — without creating yet another security group. Which Microsoft Entra feature supports this?

options.A: Microsoft Entra extension attributes 1–15 — synced from on-prem AD.
options.B: Microsoft Entra custom security attributes — administrator-defined key-value attributes assigned to users / service principals / managed identities; accessed by Conditional Access filters, application assignment filters, and (combined with PIM for Groups) eligibility scoping. Requires Attribute Definition Administrator / Attribute Assignment Administrator roles.
options.C: Microsoft Entra directory extensions on app registrations.
options.D: User account-enabled flag and signInActivity properties.

questions.rationale: Custom security attributes are a first-class Microsoft Entra capability for attribute-based access control (ABAC). They are managed by dedicated roles (Attribute Definition Administrator / Attribute Assignment Administrator) — even Global Administrator does NOT have access to them by default. They feed Conditional Access filters and app assignment filters.

optionRationales.A: Extension attributes 1–15 sync from on-prem AD and serve different scoping; they aren't the modern ABAC mechanism.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Custom security attributes are the documented ABAC feature.
optionRationales.C: Directory extensions extend object schemas via apps but aren't centrally managed by attribute admins.
optionRationales.D: User properties don't satisfy the attribute-defined-by-admin requirement.

references.text: What are custom security attributes in Microsoft Entra ID?

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.topic: Cross-tenant access settings — defaults vs per-organization
questions.question: Contoso uses Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration with dozens of partners. The security team wants 'block by default' for new partner tenants but specific elevated trust (MFA passthrough, device claim passthrough, sync settings) with two strategic partners. Which configuration achieves this?

options.A: Configure the Default settings under Cross-tenant access settings to Block all inbound/outbound, then add Organizational settings for the two strategic partners with the required allow-lists, MFA passthrough, and device claim trust.
options.B: Allow all partners by default — Conditional Access will handle restrictions per partner.
options.C: Block both default AND organizational settings; partners cannot connect.
options.D: Configure the cross-tenant access settings only on the partners' side; Contoso's side has no controls.

questions.rationale: Cross-tenant access settings define an organizational default (B2B collaboration, B2B direct connect, trust settings) plus per-tenant overrides. The block-by-default + per-partner allow model is exactly the documented enterprise pattern. Trust settings (MFA passthrough, compliant/hybrid device passthrough) require explicit per-organization configuration on both sides.

optionRationales.A: Correct. Default block + per-org allow is the documented enterprise model.
optionRationales.B: Allow-all by default contradicts the security requirement.
optionRationales.C: Block all blocks legitimate partners.
optionRationales.D: Both tenants must configure their side for trust settings (e.g., MFA passthrough) to take effect.

references.text: Configure cross-tenant access settings for B2B collaboration

questions.domainName: Implement identities in Microsoft Entra ID
questions.topic: Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync — group provisioning to AD
questions.question: An organization runs Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync (no Connect Sync) and wants cloud-created Microsoft Entra security groups to be provisioned BACK to on-premises Active Directory so file servers can ACL-enforce them. Which statement is correct?

options.A: Cloud Sync cannot provision to AD; only AD-to-Entra is supported.
options.B: Group provisioning to AD (cloud-to-AD security group sync) is a Cloud Sync exclusive feature, not available in Microsoft Entra Connect Sync. Configure it in the Cloud Sync agent + provisioning configuration in the Microsoft Entra admin center.
options.C: Only Microsoft Entra Connect Sync supports cloud-to-AD provisioning; switch to Connect.
options.D: Provisioning back to AD requires Microsoft Identity Manager (MIM).

questions.rationale: Group provisioning to AD is one of the strategic differentiating features of Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync — it is not available in Microsoft Entra Connect Sync. The decision guide lists it among the Cloud Sync-exclusive capabilities (alongside disconnected forest support, multiple active sync instances).

optionRationales.A: Group provisioning to AD IS supported in Cloud Sync.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Cloud Sync exclusive feature.
optionRationales.C: Connect Sync does NOT support cloud-to-AD user/group provisioning.
optionRationales.D: MIM is a separate legacy product, not the answer here.

references.text: Cloud Sync vs Microsoft Entra Connect feature comparison
Group provisioning to Active Directory with Cloud Sync

questions.question: An organization still uses the legacy MFA settings portal (per-user MFA) and the legacy SSPR authentication methods page. Microsoft announced these legacy settings were deprecated on September 30, 2025. Which migration approach is correct?

options.A: No migration needed — Microsoft will preserve the legacy settings indefinitely.
options.B: Migrate authentication method configuration to the unified Authentication methods policy under Microsoft Entra ID > Authentication methods > Policies. Use the migration guide to move per-method configuration (SMS, voice, Authenticator, FIDO2, passwordless phone sign-in, etc.) to the unified policy, then set the migration status to Complete to retire the legacy controls.
options.C: Disable MFA entirely; the legacy portal no longer applies.
options.D: Move to per-user MFA permanently.

questions.rationale: The Authentication methods policy is the unified control plane for all authentication methods (MFA + SSPR + passwordless). Microsoft published a migration guide and a status tracker (Migration in Progress / Migration Complete) so admins can move per-user MFA settings + legacy SSPR settings into the policy and retire the legacy portals.

optionRationales.A: Legacy settings are being retired.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Use the unified Authentication methods policy + migration guide.
optionRationales.C: Disabling MFA is the opposite of what's needed.
optionRationales.D: Per-user MFA is the legacy model being retired.

references.text: Migrate to the Authentication methods policy
Manage authentication methods in Microsoft Entra ID

questions.question: A SOC wants to enable phishing-resistant passwordless via the Microsoft Authenticator app on iOS and Android — using passkeys (device-bound FIDO2 credentials) without requiring physical security keys. Which Authentication methods policy configuration is required?

options.A: Enable the legacy 'Authenticator (Notification through mobile app)' method.
options.B: In the Authentication methods policy, enable 'Passkey (FIDO2)' and select 'Microsoft Authenticator' as an allowed provider; users register a device-bound passkey in Authenticator (iOS 17+ / Android 14+) — phishing-resistant because the credential is bound to the secure enclave / TPM.
options.C: Enable SMS as the primary authentication method.
options.D: Enable Software OATH tokens only.

questions.rationale: Microsoft Authenticator now supports device-bound passkeys (FIDO2 credentials) on iOS and Android. In the Authentication methods policy, you enable Passkey (FIDO2) with Microsoft Authenticator as an allowed provider. The credential is hardware-bound and phishing-resistant.

optionRationales.A: Authenticator notification (push) is not phishing-resistant.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Passkey (FIDO2) + Microsoft Authenticator is the documented path.
optionRationales.C: SMS is not phishing-resistant.
optionRationales.D: OATH tokens are not phishing-resistant.

questions.question: An organization's CISO requires that admin sign-ins satisfy phishing-resistant MFA. Which AUTHENTICATION METHODS qualify as phishing-resistant per Microsoft's built-in 'Phishing-resistant MFA' authentication strength?

options.A: Microsoft Authenticator push notification, voice, and SMS.
options.B: FIDO2 security keys, Windows Hello for Business, certificate-based authentication (multi-factor), and Microsoft Authenticator passkey (device-bound FIDO2). Microsoft's built-in 'Phishing-resistant MFA' authentication strength restricts satisfying methods to these.
options.C: OATH hardware tokens and Authenticator notification.
options.D: Temporary Access Pass and SMS as fallback.

questions.rationale: Microsoft's built-in 'Phishing-resistant MFA' authentication strength is satisfied by FIDO2 security keys, Windows Hello for Business, certificate-based authentication (multi-factor), and Authenticator-based passkeys. Push notifications, SMS, voice, and OATH tokens are not phishing-resistant.

optionRationales.A: Push/voice/SMS aren't phishing-resistant.
optionRationales.B: Correct. FIDO2 / Windows Hello / CBA / Authenticator passkey is the documented set.
optionRationales.C: OATH and push are not phishing-resistant.
optionRationales.D: TAP is a bootstrap mechanism, not a steady-state MFA method, and SMS is not phishing-resistant.

questions.topic: Identity Protection — confirm compromised vs dismiss user risk
questions.question: A user is flagged as High user-risk in Microsoft Entra ID Protection. After investigation, the SOC determines the activity was definitely malicious (the user's credentials were leaked). What is the appropriate action, and what does it teach the ML model?

options.A: Dismiss user risk — the issue is resolved.
options.B: Confirm compromised — marks the activity as a confirmed true positive, the user remains at risk until remediation (secure password change), and the signal trains the ML model to weight similar future signals as risky.
options.C: Delete the user account.
options.D: Reset MFA registration.

questions.rationale: Confirm compromised is the IdProtection action for true positives. It keeps the user in a risky state until they perform secure password change remediation (or admin-initiated reset), and feeds the ML model with confirmed-true-positive signal. Dismiss is for confirmed false positives (signal was incorrect).

optionRationales.A: Dismiss tells the model the signal was a false positive — wrong for a genuine compromise.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Confirm compromised is the documented response for true positives.
optionRationales.C: Deletion is over-reaction; remediation is preferred.
optionRationales.D: MFA reset alone doesn't address the credential compromise.

references.text: Investigate risk in Microsoft Entra ID Protection

questions.topic: Conditional Access — Authentication Context for sensitive operations
questions.question: A SharePoint Online site contains a 'Mergers' library. The CISO requires that opening files in that library forces a step-up to phishing-resistant MFA — but accessing other parts of SharePoint should not. Which Conditional Access feature is designed for this?

options.A: A second Conditional Access policy targeting all of SharePoint Online with Authentication Strength = Phishing-resistant MFA.
options.B: Authentication Context — define an Authentication Context value (e.g., c1: Mergers), tag the SharePoint library with that context via sensitivity label, and create a Conditional Access policy targeting the Authentication Context value with grant = Require authentication strength: Phishing-resistant MFA. Step-up triggers only when the user accesses the library.
options.C: Conditional Access — Filter for devices.
options.D: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps file policies.

questions.rationale: Authentication Context is the supported mechanism to apply Conditional Access at a sub-app granularity (specific SharePoint sites/libraries, specific OneDrive items, Power BI semantic models). Define the context, tag the resource (via sensitivity label or app config), then a CA policy targeting the context applies on resource access.

optionRationales.A: A blanket policy on all of SPO is too broad.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Authentication Context is the documented sub-app granularity mechanism.
optionRationales.C: Filter for devices targets device properties, not specific resources within an app.
optionRationales.D: MDA file policies don't enforce CA grant controls.

references.text: SharePoint sensitivity label with Authentication Context

questions.topic: Conditional Access — Sign-in frequency vs persistent browser
questions.question: An organization wants users on personal/unmanaged devices to (1) re-authenticate every 4 hours (no long-lived session), and (2) be prevented from saving the browser session as persistent. Which CA session controls deliver this?

options.A: Require compliant device only.
options.B: Conditional Access session controls: Sign-in frequency = 4 hours; Persistent browser session = Never persistent. The combination forces re-auth every 4 hours and stops the 'Stay signed in?' prompt from creating a persistent cookie.
options.C: Configure the Token lifetime policy to 4 hours.
options.D: Reduce the sign-in frequency to 90 days (default).

questions.rationale: Sign-in frequency + Persistent browser session are the documented pair of CA session controls. SIF = 4h forces re-authentication every 4 hours. Persistent browser session = Never persistent prevents the 'Stay signed in?' cookie. The legacy Token lifetime policy is being retired in favor of CA session controls.

optionRationales.A: Compliant device addresses device posture, not session duration.
optionRationales.B: Correct. SIF + Persistent browser session is the documented session control pair.
optionRationales.C: Token lifetime policies are being retired.
optionRationales.D: 90 days is the default; the requirement was 4 hours.

questions.topic: Conditional Access — devices Filter for company-owned
questions.question: A CA policy must apply ONLY to company-owned (corporate-registered) devices and NOT to BYOD devices joined to Microsoft Entra ID. Which condition expresses this most precisely?

options.A: Require Hybrid Microsoft Entra join.
options.B: Conditions > Filter for devices: include rule device.deviceOwnership -eq 'Company' (alternatively use device.isCompliant -eq True combined with Intune compliance requiring corporate enrollment).
options.C: Filter by deviceTrustType -eq 'AzureAD'.
options.D: Use Conditional Access Locations targeting 'Corporate networks'.

questions.rationale: Filter for devices supports a rich query language including device.deviceOwnership = Company/Personal, device.isCompliant, device.trustType, etc. The deviceOwnership property is the direct expression of corporate-owned. Hybrid join doesn't cover Entra-only joined corporate devices; deviceTrustType doesn't cleanly distinguish ownership.

optionRationales.A: Hybrid join is a join type, not an ownership signal.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Filter for devices with device.deviceOwnership is the documented mechanism.
optionRationales.C: trustType reflects how the device is joined, not ownership.
optionRationales.D: Locations target network, not device ownership.

questions.topic: Continuous Access Evaluation — strict location enforcement
questions.question: An organization uses Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE). They've defined a Conditional Access policy that allows access only from corporate locations. By default, CAE-aware clients can still use a previously-issued token for up to ~1 hour after leaving the corporate network. The organization wants the token to be invalidated within minutes when the user IP moves outside the trusted locations. What configuration enables this?

options.A: Disable CAE entirely.
options.B: Enable Strictly enforce location policies (Strict location enforcement) on the Conditional Access policy — CAE-aware resource providers re-evaluate the user's location on every request and revoke the token within minutes when the IP falls outside the trusted location.
options.C: Set Sign-in frequency to 5 minutes.
options.D: Disable Persistent browser session.

questions.rationale: Strict location enforcement is a CAE option that forces resource providers (Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Graph, etc.) to revalidate the user's IP against the policy on every request — drastically reducing the window in which a stolen token could be reused from outside the trusted location.

optionRationales.A: Disabling CAE reduces protection.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Strict location enforcement is the documented mechanism.
optionRationales.C: 5-minute SIF is excessive and disruptive.
optionRationales.D: Persistent browser session controls cookie persistence, not location revalidation.

questions.question: Microsoft Entra ID Protection now produces risk detections for AGENT identities (Microsoft Entra Agent ID), and Conditional Access has a new template to handle them. Which template is appropriate for blocking high-risk agents?

options.A: Require MFA for risky sign-ins — applies to agent identities too.
options.B: Microsoft-managed Conditional Access template 'Block high-risk agent identities' — targets agent identities (Preview) keyed on Agent risk = High, with grant = Block.
options.C: Block legacy authentication.
options.D: Identity Protection does not produce signals for agents.

questions.rationale: Microsoft Entra ID Protection now produces agent risk detections, and there is a dedicated Conditional Access template 'Block high-risk agent identities' that targets the Agents (Preview) assignment type with grant = Block. MFA isn't applicable to autonomous agents (no user present), so the user-targeted risk policies don't fit.

optionRationales.A: MFA doesn't apply to autonomous agents.
optionRationales.B: Correct. The 'Block high-risk agent identities' managed template is the documented control.
optionRationales.C: Legacy auth block addresses a different vector.
optionRationales.D: Agent risk detections do exist in ID Protection.

questions.topic: Microsoft-managed Conditional Access policy — Block legacy authentication
questions.question: A new tenant has security defaults disabled (the customer wants custom CA policies). Microsoft has now SHIPPED a managed Conditional Access policy that blocks legacy authentication tenant-wide. Which statement is correct about Microsoft-managed CA policies?

options.A: Microsoft-managed CA policies cannot be edited or disabled.
options.B: Microsoft-managed CA policies are pre-configured by Microsoft and appear in the customer's CA policy list in Report-only mode. The admin can review impact in sign-in logs, then move to On (or Off, or exclude specific principals). They're updated automatically by Microsoft over time.
options.C: Microsoft-managed CA policies are enabled and enforced from creation with no admin override.
options.D: Microsoft-managed CA policies only apply to E5 customers.

questions.rationale: Microsoft-managed CA policies (e.g., Block legacy authentication, Require MFA for admins accessing Microsoft admin portals, Block device code flow, MFA for all users) appear in customer tenants in Report-only mode. Admins can review impact, then enable, disable, or exclude principals. Microsoft updates them over time.

optionRationales.A: They are admin-controllable.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Microsoft-managed policies + Report-only start is the documented model.
optionRationales.C: They start in Report-only mode for review.
optionRationales.D: They are available across SKUs.

questions.topic: Global Secure Access — Microsoft 365 traffic profile
questions.question: An organization deploys Microsoft Entra Internet Access via the Global Secure Access client. They want Microsoft 365 traffic (Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Teams) to be acquired by GSA so that Conditional Access named-location and Compliant Network can be enforced — but NOT other Internet traffic for now. Which configuration is appropriate?

options.A: Enable only the Microsoft 365 traffic profile in Global Secure Access; leave Internet Access traffic profile disabled. The GSA client tunnels only M365 traffic to Microsoft's edge.
options.B: Enable all GSA traffic profiles and use a proxy PAC file to bypass non-M365.
options.C: Disable Global Secure Access; only Internet Access is supported.
options.D: Use Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps file policies instead.

questions.rationale: GSA has separate traffic profiles: Microsoft 365 traffic, Internet Access (Microsoft Entra Internet Access), and Private Access (Microsoft Entra Private Access). Each can be enabled independently. Enabling only the Microsoft 365 profile sends only Microsoft 365 traffic through GSA — useful for staged rollout and to enforce Compliant Network on M365 access first.

optionRationales.A: Correct. M365 traffic profile alone is supported.
optionRationales.B: PAC-file bypass is unnecessarily complex when profiles can be selectively enabled.
optionRationales.C: Internet Access is one option; M365 profile is another.
optionRationales.D: MDA file policies don't replace GSA traffic acquisition.

references.text: Manage Microsoft 365 traffic in Global Secure Access

questions.topic: Microsoft Entra Private Access — Quick Access vs per-app
questions.question: An organization rolls out Microsoft Entra Private Access to replace its VPN. They start with Quick Access (broad bucket of FQDNs and IP ranges). They now want per-application Conditional Access — for example, require compliant device for the HR app and phishing-resistant MFA for the finance app. Which approach is correct?

options.A: Conditional Access policies can only be applied to Quick Access as a whole.
options.B: Create per-app Global Secure Access enterprise applications: one app per internal application with its FQDN/IP segments. Each per-app application is a separate enterprise application in Microsoft Entra, so per-app Conditional Access policies (compliant device for HR app, phishing-resistant MFA for finance app) can target each one independently. Migrate apps off Quick Access progressively.
options.C: Microsoft Entra Private Access doesn't support Conditional Access.
options.D: Use Azure AD Application Proxy instead; Private Access has no per-app concept.

questions.rationale: Quick Access is a single bucket of segments shared by all users; per-app access publishes individual apps with their own FQDN/IP segments as separate enterprise applications, enabling per-app Conditional Access. The documented migration path is to gradually move from Quick Access to per-app access as apps are vetted.

optionRationales.A: Per-app CA is one of the key differentiators.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Per-app GSA enterprise apps + per-app CA is the documented mechanism.
optionRationales.C: Private Access has deep CA integration.
optionRationales.D: App Proxy is a related but separate older feature for HTTP(S) only.

references.text: Apply Conditional Access policies to Private Access apps

questions.topic: Global Secure Access — Universal Conditional Access on Internet traffic
questions.question: A security architect wants to apply Conditional Access policies (compliant device, named location, sign-in risk) to ANY internet traffic, not just authenticated SaaS sign-ins. Which Microsoft Entra capability provides this?

options.A: Conditional Access already evaluates all internet traffic by default.
options.B: Universal Conditional Access (with Microsoft Entra Internet Access via Global Secure Access) — Internet traffic acquired by the GSA client is evaluated by Conditional Access policies before reaching the destination, even for sites that don't perform Entra authentication. Enables conditions like compliant device and named location to gate non-Entra-aware traffic.
options.C: Use Azure Firewall in front of every endpoint.
options.D: Use Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps Conditional Access App Control.

questions.rationale: Universal Conditional Access is the GSA Internet Access capability that evaluates CA policies on internet-bound traffic (acquired by the GSA client) even when the destination isn't an Entra-authenticated app. Compliant Network is one of the conditions enforceable this way.

optionRationales.A: Plain CA only evaluates traffic during Entra token acquisition.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Universal CA via GSA Internet Access is the documented capability.
optionRationales.C: Azure Firewall doesn't enforce Conditional Access.
optionRationales.D: MDA App Control proxies SaaS sessions, not arbitrary internet traffic.

references.text: Universal Conditional Access in Global Secure Access

questions.question: An organization needs an authentication strength that requires (1) FIDO2 security key OR (2) Windows Hello for Business + Compliant Device — for a specific application. Which Conditional Access feature provides this combined enforcement?

options.A: There is no way to combine methods; pick one built-in authentication strength.
options.B: Create a Custom Authentication Strength that includes FIDO2 security keys AND Windows Hello for Business as allowed combinations; reference it in a Conditional Access policy. Combine with separate grant control 'Require device to be marked as compliant' to enforce device compliance for WHfB sign-ins.
options.C: Use the Microsoft-managed Phishing-resistant MFA strength — it covers this exactly.
options.D: Stack two Conditional Access policies with Block on failure.

questions.rationale: Authentication strengths can be CUSTOM — admins can define a custom strength that includes specific method combinations. Compliant Device is a separate grant control that can be combined in the same Conditional Access policy. The built-in Phishing-resistant MFA covers FIDO2, WHfB, and CBA but doesn't enforce compliant device on its own.

optionRationales.A: Custom strengths are supported.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Custom Authentication Strength + Compliant Device grant is the documented combination.
optionRationales.C: The built-in strength doesn't require compliant device.
optionRationales.D: Stacked block policies is brittle and not the documented pattern.

references.text: Conditional Access authentication strengths — custom strengths

questions.question: Which scenario is NOT an appropriate use case for Temporary Access Pass (TAP) per Microsoft's guidance?

options.A: Onboarding a new employee remotely so they can register a phishing-resistant credential without ever using a permanent password.
options.B: Re-onboarding a user whose FIDO2 security key was lost, so they can register a replacement.
options.C: Granting a long-lived (90-day) password equivalent for a service account used in scripts.
options.D: Bootstrapping Windows Hello for Business setup on a new device when the user has no password.

questions.rationale: TAP is designed for time-limited (max 30 days, default 1 hour) interactive bootstrap of strong credentials. It is NOT intended as a long-lived password equivalent for service accounts — those should use managed identities or service principals with federated credentials. The other three are documented TAP use cases.

optionRationales.A: Documented use case.
optionRationales.B: Documented use case.
optionRationales.C: Correct (NOT appropriate). TAP is not a service-account credential.
optionRationales.D: Documented use case.

references.text: Configure Temporary Access Pass in Microsoft Entra ID

questions.question: An organization wants to detect identities (users, service principals, machine identities) across Azure / AWS / GCP that hold permissions they have never exercised, and to right-size them with recommended custom roles. Which Microsoft product fits this?

options.A: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps.
options.B: Microsoft Entra Permissions Management (CIEM) — discovers identities across Azure / AWS / GCP, computes a Permission Creep Index per identity, and recommends right-sized custom roles based on observed activity over a tunable window. Integrates with Defender for Cloud as a Defender CSPM extension.
options.C: Microsoft Sentinel UEBA.
options.D: Microsoft Defender for Identity.

questions.rationale: Microsoft Entra Permissions Management is Microsoft's Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management (CIEM) product. It computes a Permission Creep Index (PCI) by comparing assigned vs used permissions, and generates right-sized role recommendations. Standalone OR integrated under Defender CSPM.

optionRationales.A: Defender for Cloud Apps targets SaaS shadow IT.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Permissions Management is the CIEM product.
optionRationales.C: Sentinel UEBA is for user behavior analytics on logs.
optionRationales.D: Defender for Identity protects on-prem AD from credential attacks.

references.text: What is Microsoft Entra Permissions Management?

questions.question: An IAM administrator wants a single dashboard showing: number of joiner/leaver workflows that ran in the past 30 days, access package assignments by status, access reviews pending decisions, and PIM activation trends. Where is this surfaced?

options.A: Microsoft 365 admin center > Reports.
options.B: Microsoft Entra admin center > Identity Governance > Dashboard — the unified view that aggregates Lifecycle Workflows, Entitlement Management, Access Reviews, and PIM telemetry into one operational picture.
options.C: Microsoft Sentinel Identity workbook.
options.D: Microsoft Entra usage and insights only.

questions.rationale: The Identity Governance dashboard in the Microsoft Entra admin center consolidates Lifecycle Workflows, Entitlement Management, Access Reviews, and PIM metrics into a single operational view for IAM administrators. Sentinel workbooks are general-purpose; the dedicated governance dashboard is the documented surface.

optionRationales.A: M365 admin center doesn't host Identity Governance dashboards.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Identity Governance > Dashboard is the documented unified surface.
optionRationales.C: Sentinel can be used but isn't the dedicated governance dashboard.
optionRationales.D: Usage and insights focuses on app sign-ins, not governance KPIs.

questions.topic: PIM — alert for roles being assigned outside of PIM
questions.question: A security architect uses PIM for all Microsoft Entra role assignments. They want to be notified IF anyone assigns a privileged role to a principal directly (bypassing PIM eligibility). Which PIM alert addresses this?

options.A: Roles assigned outside of Privileged Identity Management — fires when an admin assigns a role via Azure CLI, PowerShell, or the Microsoft Entra admin center without going through PIM.
options.B: Stale accounts in a privileged role.
options.C: Administrators aren't using their privileged roles.
options.D: Roles are being activated too frequently.

questions.rationale: PIM ships a built-in alert named 'Roles are being assigned outside of Privileged Identity Management' that fires when a role assignment is created directly (bypassing PIM eligibility/activation). The alert can be customized for severity and notified recipients in PIM > Microsoft Entra roles > Alerts.

optionRationales.A: Correct. This is the exact PIM alert name.
optionRationales.B: Stale accounts alert covers dormancy, not bypass.
optionRationales.C: Inactivity alert is different.
optionRationales.D: Frequency-based alert exists but addresses a different concern.

references.text: PIM security alerts for Microsoft Entra roles

questions.topic: Access reviews — privileged role recurring reviews
questions.question: A regulator requires quarterly review of every principal eligible for the Global Administrator role. Which Microsoft Entra capability is purpose-built for this?

options.A: Manual export from PIM and Excel review.
options.B: Create an access review of Microsoft Entra roles (or PIM-eligible role assignments) targeting Global Administrator, set frequency Quarterly, with reviewers = Self / Selected users / Group owners. Auto-apply removes non-attested assignments at quarter end.
options.C: Use PIM activation history report only.
options.D: Use Microsoft Sentinel scheduled rule querying SigninLogs.

questions.rationale: Access reviews of Microsoft Entra roles is the dedicated feature for periodic review of role-eligible principals (PIM Eligible and PIM Active). Configure frequency, reviewers, and auto-apply behavior. Requires Microsoft Entra ID P2 (Identity Protection / PIM bundle).

optionRationales.A: Manual review doesn't scale or audit.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Access reviews of Microsoft Entra roles is the documented mechanism.
optionRationales.C: Activation history reports on usage, not attestation.
optionRationales.D: Sentinel can detect but doesn't perform attestation workflows.

references.text: Access reviews of Microsoft Entra roles in PIM

questions.question: A platform engineer wants to programmatically create entitlement management catalogs and access packages as part of the org's infrastructure-as-code pipeline. Which API set is appropriate?

options.A: Microsoft Graph PowerShell — Identity Governance cmdlets (e.g., New-MgEntitlementManagementAccessPackage, New-MgEntitlementManagementAccessPackageCatalog) backed by the Microsoft Graph identityGovernance.entitlementManagement endpoints.
options.B: Azure Resource Manager templates (ARM) only.
options.C: Microsoft Entra admin center API — Graph cmdlets are not supported.
options.D: Azure DevOps REST API.

questions.rationale: Entitlement management is exposed via the Microsoft Graph identityGovernance.entitlementManagement endpoints, accessible from Microsoft Graph PowerShell (Microsoft.Graph.Identity.Governance module). ARM templates do NOT manage entitlement management — it's a Microsoft Graph resource, not an Azure Resource Manager resource.

optionRationales.A: Correct. Microsoft Graph PowerShell + Identity Governance module is the documented IaC path.
optionRationales.B: ARM doesn't manage Graph resources.
optionRationales.C: Graph cmdlets are fully supported.
optionRationales.D: Azure DevOps API is unrelated.

references.text: Manage entitlement management with Microsoft Graph PowerShell
Microsoft Graph Identity Governance API reference

questions.question: A Lifecycle Workflow Joiner workflow must, beyond the built-in tasks, also call the company's ITSM system to open a ticket and provision a non-Azure resource. Which mechanism supports this?

options.A: Lifecycle Workflows cannot be extended — only built-in tasks are supported.
options.B: Lifecycle Workflows custom task extensions — author a Logic App (or other webhook endpoint), register it as a custom task extension in the workflow, and add the extension as a task step. The extension receives workflow context (user, attribute changes) and can call the ITSM API or any external system.
options.C: Use Power Automate flows scheduled hourly.
options.D: Use Microsoft Entra Connect Sync custom rules.

questions.rationale: Lifecycle Workflows custom task extensions integrate with Azure Logic Apps (or any HTTP-based callback) so the workflow can call out to external systems at workflow execution time. This is the documented extensibility model — author the Logic App, register the extension, add it as a workflow task.

optionRationales.A: Custom task extensions are supported.
optionRationales.B: Correct. Custom task extensions via Logic Apps are the documented extensibility mechanism.
optionRationales.C: Power Automate flows aren't workflow-context-aware.
optionRationales.D: Connect Sync is unrelated.

references.text: Trigger Logic Apps from Lifecycle Workflows

questions.question: HR creates a new user account 5 days BEFORE the employee's hire date with employeeHireDate set. Which Lifecycle Workflows trigger configuration runs the Joiner tasks on Day 0 (hire date)?

options.A: Run on a schedule every hour for all users.
options.B: Configure the workflow as a Joiner with execution conditions employeeHireDate property + offsetInDays = 0 (or 'On the hire date'). The workflow runs once when the property's date matches the configured offset.
options.C: Run on user creation in the directory.
options.D: Lifecycle Workflows can't honor future dates.

questions.rationale: Lifecycle Workflows triggers can be based on Microsoft Entra attributes — `employeeHireDate` and `employeeLeaveDateTime` with an offsetInDays. Set offsetInDays = 0 for hire date day, or negative for pre-hire (e.g., -5 to run 5 days before), or positive for post-hire (e.g., 7 for the first week's tasks).

optionRationales.A: Hourly schedule isn't tied to a hire date.
optionRationales.B: Correct. employeeHireDate + offsetInDays is the documented Joiner trigger.
optionRationales.C: User creation is unrelated to hire date.
optionRationales.D: LCW supports future-dated execution via offsets.

references.text: Lifecycle Workflows — triggers and execution conditions

questions.question: An organization wants users requesting an entitlement management access package to PROVE they hold a Verified ID credential issued by a partner organization (e.g., 'Employee of Contoso') before approval. Which combination is correct?

options.A: Add a Verified ID requirement to the access package's request settings: users must present a verifiable credential matching the configured issuer and credential type as part of the request workflow.
options.B: Manually email the user and ask for a credential screenshot.
options.C: Use a SharePoint form requiring credential text upload.
options.D: Verified ID can't integrate with entitlement management.

questions.rationale: Entitlement management supports Verified ID as a request gating mechanism — configure an access package's request settings to require presentation of a verifiable credential from a specific issuer / credential type. Requested users must present a matching credential during the request flow. Combines well with cross-organization scenarios.

optionRationales.A: Correct. Verified ID as a request gate is documented integration.
optionRationales.B: Manual screenshot review is the anti-pattern.
optionRationales.C: SharePoint forms don't validate cryptographic credentials.
optionRationales.D: Verified ID + EM integration exists.

references.text: Use Verified ID with entitlement management

questions.question: An access package grants access to a SaaS app that requires a corresponding entry in the company's ITSM system. The team wants the ITSM ticket to be opened automatically when the package is GRANTED, and closed when it is REMOVED. Which mechanism supports this?

options.A: Entitlement management custom extensions — author a Logic App with two operations (grant, remove) and register it as a custom extension on the access package. The extension fires on Assignment Grant and Assignment Remove events with workflow context.
options.B: Manual coordination between IAM and IT Operations.
options.C: Microsoft Power BI dashboards.
options.D: Microsoft Sentinel automation playbooks.

questions.rationale: Entitlement management custom extensions integrate with Logic Apps to run during the access package lifecycle: on assignment grant, before/after approval, on removal. Useful for fan-out to non-Azure systems like ITSM, HR, or licensing.

optionRationales.A: Correct. Custom extensions via Logic Apps are the documented extensibility mechanism.
optionRationales.B: Manual coordination is not automation.
optionRationales.C: Power BI doesn't integrate with EM lifecycle.
optionRationales.D: Sentinel playbooks operate on security incidents, not EM grants.

questions.question: An organization migrating from on-premises Microsoft Entra Connect to cloud-first identity wants specific synchronized attributes (e.g., manager, department) to be authoritatively MANAGED IN MICROSOFT ENTRA ID going forward, even though the user still originates from AD. Which Microsoft Entra capability supports this?

options.A: Disable Microsoft Entra Connect entirely.
options.B: Source of Authority (SOA) management — a Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync feature that allows specific attributes of synced user objects to have their authoritative source moved from on-premises AD to Microsoft Entra ID, enabling cloud-first attribute management while preserving the hybrid user lifecycle.
options.C: Use Microsoft Entra extension attributes 1–15.
options.D: Use directory schema extensions.

questions.rationale: Source of Authority management is a Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync feature for cloud-first identity strategies. Specific attributes can have their authoritative source moved from on-prem AD to Entra ID — allowing cloud-side management of those attributes while keeping the user object syncing from AD overall.

optionRationales.A: Disabling Connect entirely would break the hybrid user lifecycle.
optionRationales.B: Correct. SOA management in Cloud Sync is the documented mechanism.
optionRationales.C: Extension attributes are different scoping.
optionRationales.D: Schema extensions are app-side, not SOA.

references.text: Source of Authority management with Cloud Sync
Hybrid identity scenarios — Source of Authority
